I can see how containerd handled the migration. The key insight from util_unix.go is about the scheme difference - `grpc.Dial` uses \"passthrough\" as default, while `grpc.NewClient` uses \"dns\". For custom dialers with unix sockets, we need to use the \"passthrough\" scheme explicitly.\n\nHowever, the simple example at `internal/cri/io/helpers.go:249` just shows:\n```go\nconn, err := grpc.NewClient(realAddress, gopts...)\n```\n\nSo the migration is straightforward - just replace `grpc.Dial(target, opts)` with `grpc.NewClient(target, opts)`.\n\nFor `grpc.DialContext(ctx, target, opts...)`, we need to handle the context. Looking at the examples again:\n- In `pkg/probe/grpc/grpc.go`, the context with timeout is created via `context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)` but this context is only used for the Dial call. With `grpc.NewClient`, we lose this context.\n- The solution mentioned in containerd's comments is to use dial options like `grpc.WithConnectParams()` to specify the timeout.\n\nHowever, let me think about this more carefully. Connection establishment timeout (via context.WithTimeout)\n2. Cancellation (via context.WithCancel)\n\nWith grpc.NewClient, these should be handled via dial options. Let me check if there's a way to pass context via a dial option...\n\nActually, looking at the gRPC documentation and the patterns, it seems that:\n\n\n- Connection timeouts should be replaced with `grpc.WithConnectParams()` specifying the appropriate duration\n- The context passed to DialContext was primarily for the dial phase itself, not the connection lifetime\n\nSo the straightforward replacements are: `grpc.Dial(target, opts...)` becomes `grpc.NewClient(target, opts...)`, and `grpc.DialContext(ctx, target, opts...)` also becomes `grpc.NewClient(target, opts...)` where any timeout information from the context gets moved into the dial options. \\t\\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, client.cfg.DialTimeout)\\n478: \\t}\\n479: \\terr = client.getToken(ctx)\\n480: \\tif err != nil {\\n481: \\t\\tclient.Close()\\n482: \\t\\tcancel()\\n483: \\t\\t// TODO: Consider fmt.Errorf(\\\"communicating with [%s] failed: %v\\\", strings.Join(cfg.Endpoints, \\\";\\\"), err)\\n484: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n485: \\t}\\n486: \\tcancel()\\n487: \\n488: \\tif cfg.RejectOldCluster {\\n489: \\t\\tif err := client.checkVersion(); err != nil {\\n490: \\t\\t\\tclient.Close()\\n491: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n492: \\t\\t}\\n493: \\t}\\n494: \\n495: \\tgo client.autoSync()\\n496: \\treturn client, nil\\n497: }\\n498: \\n499: // roundRobinQuorumBackoff retries against quorum between each backoff.\\n500: // This is intended for use with a round robin load balancer.\\n501: func (c *Client) roundRobinQuorumBackoff(waitBetween time.Duration, jitterFraction float64) backoffFunc {\\n502: \\treturn func(attempt uint) time.Duration {\\n503: \\t\\t// after each round robin across quorum, backoff for our wait between duration\\n504: \\t\\tn := uint(len(c.Endpoints()))\\n505: \\t\\tquorum := (n/2 + 1)\\n506: \\t\\tif attempt%quorum == 0 {\\n507: \\t\\t\\tc.GetLogger().Debug(\\\"backoff\\\", zap.Uint(\\\"attempt\\\", attempt), zap.Uint(\\\"quorum\\\", quorum), zap.Duration(\\\"waitBetween\\\", waitBetween), zap.Float64(\\\"jitterFraction\\\", jitterFraction))\\n508: \\t\\t\\treturn jitterUp(waitBetween, jitterFraction)\\n509: \\t\\t}\\n510: \\t\\tc.GetLogger().Debug(\\\"backoff skipped\\\", zap.Uint(\\\"attempt\\\", attempt), zap.Uint(\\\"quorum\\\", quorum))\\n511: \\t\\treturn 0\\n512: \\t}\\n513: }\\n514: \\n515: // minSupportedVersion returns the minimum version supported, which is the previous minor release.\\n516: func minSupportedVersion() *semver.Version {\\n517: \\tver := semver.Must(semver.NewVersion(version.Version))\\n518: \\t// consider only major and minor version\\n519: \\tver = &semver.Version{Major: ver.Major, Minor: ver.Minor}\\n520: \\tfor i := range version.AllVersions {\\n521: \\t\\tif version.AllVersions[i].Equal(*ver) {\\n522: \\t\\t\\tif i == 0 {\\n523: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ver\\n524: \\t\\t\\t}\\n525: \\t\\t\\treturn &version.AllVersions[i-1]\\n526: \\t\\t}\\n527: \\t}\\n528: \\tpanic(\\\"current version is not in the version list\\\")\\n529: }\\n530: \\n531: func (c *Client) checkVersion() (err error) {\\n532: \\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\\n533: \\n534: \\teps := c.Endpoints()\\n535: \\terrc := make(chan error, len(eps))\\n536: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(c.ctx)\\n537: \\tif c.cfg.DialTimeout > 0 {\\n538: \\t\\tcancel()\\n539: \\t\\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(c.ctx, c.cfg.DialTimeout)\\n540: \\t}\\n541: \\n542: \\twg.Add(len(eps))\\n543: \\tfor _, ep := range eps {\\n544: \\t\\t// if cluster is current, any endpoint gives a recent version\\n545: \\t\\tgo func(e string) {\\n546: \\t\\t\\tdefer wg.Done()\\n547: \\t\\t\\tresp, rerr := c.Status(ctx, e)\\n548: \\t\\t\\tif rerr != nil {\\n549: \\t\\t\\t\\terrc <- rerr\\n550: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n551: \\t\\t\\t}\\n552: \\t\\t\\tvs, serr := semver.NewVersion(resp.Version)\\n553: \\t\\t\\tif serr != nil {\\n554: \\t\\t\\t\\terrc <- serr\\n555: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n556: \\t\\t\\t}\\n557: \\n558: \\t\\t\\tif ActiveConnection returns the current in-use connection\\n576: func (c *Client) ActiveConnection() *grpc.ClientConn { return c.conn }\\n577: \\n578: // isHaltErr returns true if the given error and context indicate no forward\\n579: // progress can be made, even after reconnecting.\\n580: func isHaltErr(ctx context.Context, err error) bool {\\n581: \\tif ctx != nil && ctx.Err() != nil {\\n582: \\t\\treturn true\\n583: \\t}\\n584: \\tif err == nil {\\n585: \\t\\treturn false\\n586: \\t}\\n587: \\tev, _ := status.FromError(err)\\n588: \\t// Unavailable codes mean the system will be right back.\\n589: \\t// (e.g., can't connect, lost leader)\\n590: \\t// Treat Internal codes as if something failed, leaving the\\n591: \\t// system in an inconsistent state, but retrying could make progress.\\n592: \\t// (e.g., failed in middle of send, corrupted frame)\\n593: \\t// TODO: are permanent Internal errors possible from grpc?\\n594: \\treturn ev.Code() != codes.Unavailable && ev.Code() != codes.Internal\\n595: }\\n596: \\n597: // isUnavailableErr returns true if the given error is an unavailable error\\n598: func isUnavailableErr(ctx context.Context, err error) bool {\\n599: \\tif ctx != nil && ctx.Err() != nil {\\n600: \\t\\treturn false\\n601: \\t}\\n602: \\tif err == nil {\\n603: \\t\\treturn false\\n604: \\t}\\n605: \\tev, ok := status.FromError(err)\\n606: \\t}\\n611: \\treturn false\\n612: }\\n613: \\n614: // ContextError converts the error into an EtcdError if the error message matches one of\\n615: // the defined messages; otherwise, it tries to retrieve the context error.\\n616: func ContextError(ctx context.Context, err error) error {\\n617: \\tif err == nil {\\n618: \\t\\treturn nil\\n619: \\t}\\n620: \\terr = rpctypes.Error(err)\\n621: \\tvar serverErr rpctypes.EtcdError\\n622: \\tif errors.As(err, &serverErr) {\\n623: \\t\\treturn err\\n624: \\t}\\n625: \\tif ev, ok := status.FromError(err); ok {\\n626: \\t\\tcode := // IsConnCanceled returns true, if error is from a closed gRPC connection.\\n648: // ref. https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/pull/1854\\n649: func IsConnCanceled(err error) bool {\\n650: \\tif err == nil {\\n651: \\t\\treturn false\\n652: \\t}\\n653: \\n654: \\t// >= gRPC v1.23.x\\n655: \\ts, ok := status.FromError(err)\\n656: \\tif ok {\\n657: \\t\\t// connection is canceled or server has already nil\\n339: }\\n340: \\n341: func authority(endpoint string) string {\\n342: \\tspl := strings.SplitN(endpoint, \\\"://\\\", 2)\\n343: \\tif len(spl) < 2 {\\n344: \\t\\tif strings.HasPrefix(endpoint, (*Client, error) {\\n373: \\tif cfg == nil {\\n374: \\t\\tcfg = &Config{}\\n375: \\t}\\n376: \\tvar creds grpccredentials.TransportCredentials\\n377: \\tif cfg.TLS != nil {\\n378: \\t\\tcreds = \\t\\tclient.Token = cfg.Token\\n427: \\t\\tclient.authTokenBundle = credentials.NewPerRPCCredentialBundle()\\n428: \\t}\\n429: \\n430: \\tif cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize > 0 || cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize > 0 \\t\\t}\\n445: \\t\\tclient.callOpts = callOpts\\n446: \\t}\\n447: \\n448: \\tclient.resolver = resolver.New(cfg.Endpoints...)\\n449: \\n450: \\tif len(cfg.Endpoints) < 1 {\\n451: \\t\\tclient.cancel()\\n452: \\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"at least one Endpoint is required in client config\\\")\\n453: \\t}\\n454: \\tclient.SetEndpoints(cfg.Endpoints...)\\n455: \\n456: \\t// Use a provided endpoint target so that for https:// without any tls config given, then\\n457: \\t// grpc will assume the certificate server name is the endpoint host.\\n458: \\tconn, err := \\tclient.Maintenance = NewMaintenance(client)\\n473: \\n474: \\t// get token with established connection\\n475: \\tctx, cancel = client.ctx, func() {}\\n476: \\tif client.cfg.DialTimeout > 0 {\\n477: client.autoSync()\\n496: \\treturn client, nil\\n497: }\\n498: \\n499: // roundRobinQuorumBackoff retries against quorum between each backoff.\\n500: // This is intended for use with a round robin load balancer.\\n501: func (c *Client) roundRobinQuorumBackoff(waitBetween time.Duration, jitterFraction float64) backoffFunc {\\n502: \\treturn func(attempt uint) time.Duration {\\n503: \\t\\t// after each round robin across quorum, backoff for our wait between duration\\n504: \\t\\tn := uint(len(c.Endpoints()))\\n505: \\t\\tquorum := (n/2 + 1)\\n506: \\t\\tif attempt%quorum == 0 {\\n507: func minSupportedVersion() *semver.Version {\\n517: \\tver := semver.Must(semver.NewVersion(version.Version))\\n518: \\t// consider only major and minor version\\n519: \\tver = &semver.Version{Major: ver.Major, Minor: ver.Minor}\\n520: \\tfor i := range version.AllVersions {\\n521: \\t\\tif version.AllVersions[i].Equal(*ver) {\\n522: \\t\\t\\tif i == 0 {\\n523: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ver\\n524: checkVersion() (err error) {\\n532: \\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\\n533: \\n534: \\teps := c.Endpoints()\\n535: \\terrc := make(chan error, len(eps))\\n536: \\tctx, cancel := context indicate no forward\\n579: // progress can be made, even after reconnecting.\\n580: func isHaltErr(ctx context.Context, err error) bool {\\n581: \\tif ctx != nil && ctx.Err() != nil {\\n582: \\t\\treturn true\\n583: \\t}\\n584: \\tif err == nil {\\n585: \\t\\treturn false\\n586: \\t}\\n587: \\tev, _ := status.FromError(err)\\n588: \\t// Unavailable codes mean the system will be right back.\\n589: \\t// (e.g., can't connect, lost leader)\\n590: \\t// Treat Internal codes as if something failed, leaving the\\n591: \\t// system in an inconsistent state, but retrying could make progress.\\n592: \\t// (e.g., failed in middle of send, corrupted frame)\\n593: \\t// TODO: are permanent Internal errors possible from grpc?\\n594: \\treturn ev.Code() != codes.Unavailable && ev.Code() != codes.Internal\\n595: }\\n596: \\n597: // isUnavailableErr returns true if the given error is an unavailable error\\n598: func isUnavailableErr(ctx context.Context, err error) bool \\t}\\n611: \\treturn false\\n612: }\\n613: \\n614: // ContextError converts the error into an EtcdError if the error message matches one of\\n615: // the defined messages; otherwise, it tries to retrieve the context error.\\n616: func ContextError(ctx context.Context, err error) error {\\n617: \\tif err == nil {\\n618: \\t\\treturn nil\\n619: \\t}\\n620: \\terr = rpctypes.Error(err)\\n621: \\tvar serverErr rpctypes.EtcdError\\n622: \\tif errors.As(err, &serverErr) {\\n623: \\t\\treturn err\\n624: \\t}\\n625: \\tif ev, ok := status.FromError(err); ok {\\n626: \\t\\tcode := // IsConnCanceled returns true, if error is from a closed gRPC connection.\\n648: // ref. https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/pull/1854\\n649: func IsConnCanceled(err error) bool {\\n650: \\tif err == nil endpoints from the etcd membership.\n186: func (c *Client) Sync(ctx context.Context) error {\n187: \tmresp, err := c.MemberList(ctx)\n188: \tif err != nil {\n189: \t\treturn err\n190: \t}\n191: \tvar `MemberList` returned successfully, so the\n198: \t// endpoints shouldn't be empty.\n199: \tverify.Verify(\"empty endpoints returned from etcd cluster\", func() (bool, map[string]any) {\n200: \t\treturn len(eps) > 0, nil\n201: \t})\n202: \tc.SetEndpoints(eps...)\n203: \tc.GetLogger().Debug(\"set etcd endpoints by autoSync\", zap.Strings(\"endpoints\", eps))\n204: \treturn nil\n205: }\n206: \n207: func (c *Client) autoSync() {\n208: \tif c.cfg.AutoSyncInterval == time.Duration(0) {\n209: \t\treturn\n210: \t}\n211: \n212: \tfor {\n213: \t\tselect {\n214: \t\tcase <-c.ctx.Done():\n215: \t\t\treturn\n216: \t\tcase <-time.After(c.cfg.AutoSyncInterval):\n217: \t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(c.ctx, 5*time.Second)\n218: \t\t\terr := c.Sync(ctx)\n219: \t\t\tcancel()\n220: \t}\n239: \topts = append(opts, dopts...)\n240: \n241: \tif creds != nil {\n242: \t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(creds))\n243: \t} else {\n244: \t\topts = append(opts, c.cfg.MaxUnaryRetries\n250: \t}\n251: \n252: \tbackoffWaitBetween := defaultBackoffWaitBetween\n253: \tif c.cfg.BackoffWaitBetween > 0 {\n254: \t\tbackoffWaitBetween = c.cfg.BackoffWaitBetween\n255: \t}\n256: \n257: \tbackoffJitterFraction := defaultBackoffJitterFraction\n258: \tif c.cfg.BackoffJitterFraction > 0 {\n259: \t\tbackoffJitterFraction = c.cfg.BackoffJitterFraction\n260: \t}\n261: \n262: \t// Interceptor retry and backoff.\n263: \t// TODO: Replace all of clientv3/retry.go with RetryPolicy:\n264: \t// https://github.com/grpc/grpc-proto/blob/cdd9ed5c3d3f87aef62f373b93361cf7bddc620d/grpc/service_config/service_config.proto#L130\n265: \trrBackoff := withBackoff(c.roundRobinQuorumBackoff(backoffWaitBetween, backoffJitterFraction))\n266: \topts = append(opts,\n267: \t\t// Disable stream retry by default since go-grpc-middleware/retry does not support client streams.\n268: \t\t// Streams that are safe to retry are enabled individually.\n269: \t\tgrpc.WithStreamInterceptor(c.streamClientInterceptor(withMax(0), rrBackoff)),\n270: round robin load balancer.\n501: func (c *Client) roundRobinQuorumBackoff(waitBetween time.Duration, jitterFraction float64) backoffFunc {\n502: \treturn func(attempt uint) time.Duration {\n503: \t\t// after each round robin across quorum, backoff for our wait between duration\n504: \t\tn := uint(len(c.Endpoints()))\n505: \t\tquorum := (n/2 + 1)\n506: \t\tif attempt%quorum == 0 {\n507: \t\t\treturn jitterUp(waitBetween, jitterFraction)\n509: \t\t}\n510: \t\tc.GetLogger().Debug(\"backoff skipped\", zap.Uint(\"attempt\", attempt), zap.Uint(\"quorum\", quorum))\n511: \t\treturn 0\n512: \t}\n513: }\n514: \n515: // minSupportedVersion returns the minimum version supported, which is the previous minor release.\n516: func minSupportedVersion() *semver.Version {\n517: \tver := (*Client, error) {\n\tif len(cfg.Endpoints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrNoAvailableEndpoints\n\t}\n\n\treturn newClient(&cfg)\n}\n\n// NewCtxClient creates a client with a context but no underlying grpc\n// connection. This is useful for embedded cases that override the\n// service interface implementations and do not need connection management.\nfunc NewCtxClient(ctx context.Context, opts ...Option) *Client {\n\tcctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tc := &Client{ctx: cctx, cancel: cancel, epMu: new(sync.RWMutex)}\n\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n\t\topt(c)\n\t}\n\tif c.lg.Load() == nil {\n\t\tc.lg.Store(zap.NewNop())\n\t}\n\treturn c\n}\n\n// Option is a function type that can be passed as argument to NewCtxClient to configure client\ntype Option func(*Client)\n\n// NewFromURL creates a new etcdv3 client from a URL.\nfunc NewFromURL(url string) (*Client, error) {\n\treturn New(Config{Endpoints: []string{url}})\n}\n\n// NewFromURLs creates a new etcdv3 client from URLs.\nfunc NewFromURLs(urls []string) (*Client, error) {\n\treturn New(Config{Endpoints: urls})\n}\n\n// WithZapLogger is a NewCtxClient option that overrides the logger\nfunc WithZapLogger(lg *zap.Logger) Option {\n\treturn func(c *Client) {\n\t\tc.lg.Store(lg)\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithLogger overrides the logger.\n//\n// Deprecated: Please use WithZapLogger or Logger field in clientv3.Config\n//\n// Does not changes grpcLogger, that can be explicitly configured\n// using grpc_zap.ReplaceGrpcLoggerV2(..) method.\nfunc (c *Client) WithLogger(lg *zap.Logger) *Client {\n\tc.lg.Store(lg)\n\treturn c\n}\n\n// GetLogger gets the logger.\n// NOTE: This method is for internal use of etcd-client library and should not be used as general-purpose logger.\nfunc (c *Client) GetLogger() *zap.Logger {\n\treturn c.lg.Load()\n}\n\n// Close shuts down the client's etcd connections.\nfunc (c *Client) Close() error {\n\tc.cancel()\n\tif c.Watcher != nil {\n\t\tc.Watcher.Close()\n\t}\n\tif c.Lease != nil {\n\t\tc.Lease.Close()\n\t}\n\tif c.conn != nil {\n\t\treturn ContextError(c.ctx, c.conn.Close())\n\t}\n\treturn c.ctx.Err()\n}\n\n// Ctx is a context for \"out of band\" messages (e.g., for sending\n// \"clean up\" message when another context is canceled). It is\n// canceled on client Close().\nfunc (c *Client) Ctx() context.Context { return c.ctx }\n\n// Endpoints lists the registered endpoints for the client.\nfunc (c *Client) Endpoints() []string {\n\t// copy the slice; protect original endpoints from being changed\n\tc.epMu.RLock()\n\tdefer c.epMu.RUnlock()\n\teps := make([]string, len(c.endpoints))\n\tcopy(eps, c.endpoints)\n\treturn eps\n}\n\n// SetEndpoints updates client's endpoints.\nfunc (c *Client) SetEndpoints(eps ...string) {\n\tc.epMu.Lock()\n\tdefer c.epMu.Unlock()\n\tc.endpoints = eps\n\n\tc.resolver.SetEndpoints(eps)\n}\n\n// Sync synchronizes client's endpoints with the known endpoints from the etcd membership.\nfunc (c *Client) Sync(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tmresp, err := c.MemberList(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tvar eps []string\n\tfor _, m := range mresp.Members {\n\t\tif len(m.Name) != 0 && !m.IsLearner {\n\t\t\teps = append(eps, m.ClientURLs...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// The linearizable `MemberList` returned successfully, so the\n\t// endpoints shouldn't be empty.\n\tverify.Verify(\"empty endpoints returned from etcd cluster\", func() (bool, map[string]any) {\n\t\treturn len(eps) > 0, nil\n\t})\n\tc.SetEndpoints(eps...)\n\tc.GetLogger().Debug(\"set etcd endpoints by autoSync\", zap.Strings(\"endpoints\", eps))\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) autoSync() {\n\tif c.cfg.AutoSyncInterval == time.Duration(0) {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-c.ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-time.After(c.cfg.AutoSyncInterval):\n\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(c.ctx, 5*time.Second)\n\t\t\terr := c.Sync(ctx)\n\t\t\tcancel()\n\t\t\tif err != nil && !errors.Is(err, c.ctx.Err()) {\n\t\t\t\tc.GetLogger().Info(\"Auto sync endpoints failed.\", zap.Error(err))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// dialSetupOpts gives the dial opts prior to any authentication.\nfunc (c *Client) dialSetupOpts(creds grpccredentials.TransportCredentials, dopts ...grpc.DialOption) []grpc.DialOption {\n\tvar opts []grpc.DialOption\n\n\tif c.cfg.DialKeepAliveTime > 0 {\n\t\tparams := keepalive.ClientParameters{\n\t\t\tTime:                c.cfg.DialKeepAliveTime,\n\t\t\tTimeout:             c.cfg.DialKeepAliveTimeout,\n\t\t\tPermitWithoutStream: c.cfg.PermitWithoutStream,\n\t\t}\n\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithKeepaliveParams(params))\n\t}\n\topts = append(opts, dopts...)\n\n\tif creds != nil {\n\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(creds))\n\t} else {\n\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))\n\t}\n\n\tunaryMaxRetries := defaultUnaryMaxRetries\n\tif c.cfg.MaxUnaryRetries > 0 {\n\t\tunaryMaxRetries = c.cfg.MaxUnaryRetries\n\t}\n\n\tbackoffWaitBetween := defaultBackoffWaitBetween\n\tif c.cfg.BackoffWaitBetween > 0 {\n\t\tbackoffWaitBetween = c.cfg.BackoffWaitBetween\n\t}\n\n\tbackoffJitterFraction := defaultBackoffJitterFraction\n\tif c.cfg.BackoffJitterFraction > 0 {\n\t\tbackoffJitterFraction = c.cfg.BackoffJitterFraction\n\t}\n\n\t// Interceptor retry and backoff.\n\t// TODO: Replace all of clientv3/retry.go with RetryPolicy:\n\t// https://github.com/grpc/grpc-proto/blob/cdd9ed5c3d3f87aef62f373b93361cf7bddc620d/grpc/service_config/service_config.proto#L130\n\trrBackoff := withBackoff(c.roundRobinQuorumBackoff(backoffWaitBetween, backoffJitterFraction))\n\topts = append(opts,\n\t\t// Disable stream retry by default since go-grpc-middleware/retry does not support client streams.\n\t\t// Streams that are safe to retry are enabled individually.\n\t\tgrpc.WithStreamInterceptor(c.streamClientInterceptor(withMax(0), rrBackoff)),\n\t\tgrpc.WithUnaryInterceptor(c.unaryClientInterceptor(withMax(unaryMaxRetries), rrBackoff)),\n\t)\n\n\treturn opts\n}\n\n// Dial connects to a single endpoint using the client's config.\nfunc (c *Client) Dial(ep string) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n\tcreds := c.credentialsForEndpoint(ep)\n\n\t// Using ad-hoc created resolver, to guarantee only explicitly given\n\t// endpoint is used.\n\treturn c.dial(creds, grpc.WithResolvers(resolver.New(ep)))\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) getToken(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tvar err error // return last error in a case of fail\n\n\tif c.Token != \"\" {\n\t\tc.authTokenBundle.UpdateAuthToken(c.Token)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif c.Username == \"\" || c.Password == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tresp, err := c.Auth.Authenticate(ctx, c.Username, c.Password)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, rpctypes.ErrAuthNotEnabled) {\n\t\t\tc.authTokenBundle.UpdateAuthToken(\"\")\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tc.authTokenBundle.UpdateAuthToken(resp.Token)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// dialWithBalancer dials the client's current load balanced resolver group.  The scheme of the host\n// of the provided endpoint determines the scheme used for all endpoints of the client connection.\nfunc (c *Client) dialWithBalancer(dopts ...grpc.DialOption) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n\tcreds := c.credentialsForEndpoint(c.Endpoints()[0])\n\topts := append(dopts, grpc.WithResolvers(c.resolver))\n\treturn c.dial(creds, opts...)\n}\n\n// dial configures and dials any grpc balancer target.\nfunc (c *Client) dial(creds grpccredentials.TransportCredentials, dopts ...grpc.DialOption) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n\topts := c.dialSetupOpts(creds, dopts...)\n\n\tif c.authTokenBundle != nil {\n\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithPerRPCCredentials(c.authTokenBundle.PerRPCCredentials()))\n\t}\n\n\topts = append(opts, c.cfg.DialOptions...)\n\n\ttarget := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s://%p/%s\", resolver.Schema, c, authority(c.endpoints[0]))\n\tconn, err := grpc.NewClient(target, opts...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\treturn conn, nil\n}\n\nfunc authority(endpoint string) string {\n\tspl := strings.SplitN(endpoint, \"://\", 2)\n\tif len(spl) < 2 {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(endpoint, \"unix:\") {\n\t\t\treturn endpoint[len(\"unix:\"):]\n\t\t}\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(endpoint, \"unixs:\") {\n\t\t\treturn endpoint[len(\"unixs:\"):]\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn endpoint\n\t}\n\treturn spl[1]\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) credentialsForEndpoint(ep string) grpccredentials.TransportCredentials {\n\tr := endpoint.RequiresCredentials(ep)\n\tswitch r {\n\tcase endpoint.CredsDrop:\n\t\treturn nil\n\tcase endpoint.CredsOptional:\n\t\treturn c.creds\n\tcase endpoint.CredsRequire:\n\t\tif c.creds != nil {\n\t\t\treturn c.creds\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn credentials.NewTransportCredential(nil)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported CredsRequirement: %v\", r))\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc newClient(cfg *Config) (*Client, error) {\n\tif cfg == nil {\n\t\tcfg = &Config{}\n\t}\n\tvar creds grpccredentials.TransportCredentials\n\tif cfg.TLS != nil {\n\t\tcreds = credentials.NewTransportCredential(cfg.TLS)\n\t}\n\n\tif cfg.Token != \"\" && (cfg.Username != \"\" || cfg.Password != \"\") {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMutuallyExclusiveCfg\n\t}\n\n\t// use a temporary skeleton client to bootstrap first connection\n\tbaseCtx := context.TODO()\n\tif cfg.Context != nil {\n\t\tbaseCtx = cfg.Context\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(baseCtx)\n\tclient := &Client{\n\t\tconn:     nil,\n\t\tcfg:      *cfg,\n\t\tcreds:    creds,\n\t\tctx:      ctx,\n\t\tcancel:   cancel,\n\t\tepMu:     new(sync.RWMutex),\n\t\tcallOpts: defaultCallOpts,\n\t}\n\n\tvar err error\n\tvar lg *zap.Logger\n\tif cfg.Logger != nil {\n\t\tlg = cfg.Logger\n\t} else if cfg.LogConfig != nil {\n\t\tlg, err = cfg.LogConfig.Build()\n\t} else {\n\t\tlg, err = logutil.CreateDefaultZapLogger(ClientLogLevel())\n\t\tif lg != nil {\n\t\t\tlg = lg.Named(\"etcd-client\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tclient.lg.Store(lg)\n\n\tif cfg.Username != \"\" && cfg.Password != \"\" {\n\t\tclient.Username = cfg.Username\n\t\tclient.Password = cfg.Password\n\t\tclient.authTokenBundle = credentials.NewPerRPCCredentialBundle()\n\t}\n\n\tif cfg.Token != \"\" {\n\t\tclient.Token = cfg.Token\n\t\tclient.authTokenBundle = credentials.NewPerRPCCredentialBundle()\n\t}\n\n\tif cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize > 0 || cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize > 0 {\n\t\tif cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize > 0 && cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize > cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"gRPC message recv limit (%d bytes) must be greater than send limit (%d bytes)\", cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize, cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcallOpts := []grpc.CallOption{\n\t\t\tdefaultWaitForReady,\n\t\t\tdefaultMaxCallSendMsgSize,\n\t\t\tdefaultMaxCallRecvMsgSize,\n\t\t}\n\t\tif cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize > 0 {\n\t\t\tcallOpts[1] = grpc.MaxCallSendMsgSize(cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize > 0 {\n\t\t\tcallOpts[2] = grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize)\n\t\t}\n\t\tclient.callOpts = callOpts\n\t}\n\n\tclient.resolver = resolver.New(cfg.Endpoints...)\n\n\tif len(cfg.Endpoints) < 1 {\n\t\tclient.cancel()\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"at least one Endpoint is required in client config\")\n\t}\n\tclient.SetEndpoints(cfg.Endpoints...)\n\n\t// Use a provided endpoint target so that for https:// without any tls config given, then\n\t// grpc will assume the certificate server name is the endpoint host.\n\tconn, err := client.dialWithBalancer()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tclient.cancel()\n\t\tclient.resolver.Close()\n\t\t// TODO: Error like `fmt.Errorf(dialing [%s] failed: %v, strings.Join(cfg.Endpoints, \\\";\\\"), err)` would help with debugging a lot.\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tclient.conn = conn\n\n\tclient.Cluster = NewCluster(client)\n\tclient.KV = NewKV(client)\n\tclient.Lease = NewLease(client)\n\tclient.Watcher = NewWatcher(client)\n\tclient.Auth = NewAuth(client)\n\tclient.Maintenance = NewMaintenance(client)\n\n\t// get token with established connection\n\tctx, cancel = client.ctx, func() {}\n\tif client.cfg.DialTimeout > 0 {\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, client.cfg.DialTimeout)\n\t}\n\terr = client.getToken(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tclient.Close()\n\t\tcancel()\n\t\t// TODO: Consider fmt.Errorf(\\\"communicating with [%s] failed: %v\\\", strings.Join(cfg.Endpoints, \\\";\\\"), err)\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tcancel()\n\n\tif cfg.RejectOldCluster {\n\t\tif err := client.checkVersion(); err != nil {\n\t\t\tclient.Close()\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tgo client.autoSync()\n\treturn client, nil\n}\n\n// roundRobinQuorumBackoff retries against quorum between each backoff.\n// This is intended for use with a round robin load balancer.\nfunc (c *Client) roundRobinQuorumBackoff(waitBetween time.Duration, jitterFraction float64) backoffFunc {\n\treturn func(attempt uint) time.Duration {\n\t\t// after each round robin across quorum, backoff for our wait between duration\n\t\tn := uint(len(c.Endpoints()))\n\t\tquorum := (n/2 + 1)\n\t\tif attempt%quorum == 0 {\n\t\t\tc.GetLogger().Debug(\"backoff\", zap.Uint(\"attempt\", attempt), zap.Uint(\"quorum\", quorum), zap.Duration(\"waitBetween\", waitBetween), zap.Float64(\"jitterFraction\", jitterFraction))\n\t\t\treturn jitterUp(waitBetween, jitterFraction)\n\t\t}\n\t\tc.GetLogger().Debug(\"backoff skipped\", zap.Uint(\"attempt\", attempt), zap.Uint(\"quorum\", quorum))\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n}\n\n// minSupportedVersion returns the minimum version supported, which is the previous minor release.\nfunc minSupportedVersion() *semver.Version {\n\tver := semver.Must(semver.NewVersion(version.Version))\n\t// consider only major and minor version\n\tver = &semver.Version{Major: ver.Major, Minor: ver.Minor}\n\tfor i := range version.AllVersions {\n\t\tif version.AllVersions[i].Equal(*ver) {\n\t\t\tif i == 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn ver\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn &version.AllVersions[i-1]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tpanic(\"current version is not in the version list\")\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) checkVersion() (err error) {\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\n\teps := c.Endpoints()\n\tterrc := make(chan error, len(eps))\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(c.ctx)\n\tif c.cfg.DialTimeout > 0 {\n\t\tcancel()\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(c.ctx, c.cfg.DialTimeout)\n\t}\n\n\twg.Add(len(eps))\n\tfor _, ep := range eps {\n\t\t// if cluster is current, any endpoint gives a recent version\n\t\tgo func(e string) {\n\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n\t\t\tresp, rerr := c.Status(ctx, e)\n\t\t\tif rerr != nil {\n\t\t\t\tterrc <- rerr\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tvs, serr := semver.NewVersion(resp.Version)\n\t\t\tif serr != nil {\n\t\t\t\tterrc <- serr\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif vs.LessThan(*minSupportedVersion()) {\n\t\t\t\trerr = ErrOldCluster\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tterrc <- rerr\n\t\t}(ep)\n\t}\n\t// wait for success\n\tfor range eps {\n\t\tif err = <-terrc; err != nil {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tcancel()\n\twg.Wait()\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// ActiveConnection returns the current in-use connection\nfunc (c *Client) ActiveConnection() *grpc.ClientConn { return c.conn }\n\n// isHaltErr returns true if the given error and context indicate no forward\n// progress can be made, even after reconnecting.\nfunc isHaltErr(ctx context.Context, err error) bool {\n\tif ctx != nil && ctx.Err() != nil {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tev, _ := status.FromError(err)\n\t// Unavailable codes mean the system will be right back.\n\t// (e.g., can't connect, lost leader)\n\t// Treat Internal codes as if something failed, leaving the\n\t// system in an inconsistent state, but retrying could make progress.\n\t// (e.g., failed in middle of send, corrupted frame)\n\t// TODO: are permanent Internal errors possible from grpc?\n\treturn ev.Code() != codes.Unavailable && ev.Code() != codes.Internal\n}\n\n// isUnavailableErr returns true if the given error is an unavailable error\nfunc isUnavailableErr(ctx context.Context, err error) bool {\n\tif ctx != nil && ctx.Err() != nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tev, ok := status.FromError(err)\n\tif ok {\n\t\t// Unavailable codes mean the system will be right back.\n\t\t// (e.g., can't connect, lost leader)\n\t\treturn ev.Code() == codes.Unavailable\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// ContextError converts the error into an EtcdError if the error message matches one of\n// the defined messages; otherwise, it tries to retrieve the context error.\nfunc ContextError(ctx context.Context, err error) error {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\terr = rpctypes.Error(err)\n\tvar serverErr rpctypes.EtcdError\n\tif errors.As(err, &serverErr) {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif ev, ok := status.FromError(err); ok {\n\t\tcode := ev.Code()\n\t\tswitch code {\n\t\tcase codes.DeadlineExceeded:\n\t\t\tfallthrough\n\t\tcase codes.Canceled:\n\t\t\tif ctx.Err() != nil {\n\t\t\t\terr = ctx.Err()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn err\n}\n\nfunc canceledByCaller(stopCtx context.Context, err error) bool {\n\tif stopCtx.Err() == nil || err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded)\n}\n\n// IsConnCanceled returns true, if error is from a closed gRPC connection.\n// ref. https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/pull/1854\nfunc IsConnCanceled(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// >= gRPC v1.23.x\n\ts, ok := status.FromError(err)\n\tif ok {\n\t\t// connection is canceled or server has already closed the connection\n\t\treturn s.Code() == codes.Canceled || s.Message() == \"transport is closing\"\n\t}\n\n\t// >= gRPC v1.10.x\n\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\t// <= gRPC v1.7.x returns 'errors.New(\"grpc: the client connection is closing\")'\n\treturn strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"grpc: the client connection is closing\")\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":9862,"cache_read_input_tokens":71812,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":9862},"output_tokens":4,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e01e63bb-6150-41cb-a901-ad13de0f0415","uuid":"14220c17-82cb-44fb-9fe1-06101446d578"}

{\n\tCluster\n\tKV\n\tLease\n\tWatcher\n\tAuth\n\tMaintenance\n\n\tconn *grpc.ClientConn\n\n\tcfg      Config\n\tcreds    grpccredentials.TransportCredentials\n\tresolver (*Client, error) {\n\tif len(cfg.Endpoints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrNoAvailableEndpoints\n\t}\n\n\treturn newClient(&cfg)\n}\n\n// NewCtxClient creates a client with a context but no underlying grpc\n// connection. {\n\tcctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tc := &Client{ctx: cctx, cancel: cancel, epMu: new(sync.RWMutex)}\n\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n\t\topt(c)\n\t}\n\tif c.lg.Load() == nil {\n\tc.lg.Store(lg)\n\treturn c\n}\n\n// GetLogger gets the logger.\n// NOTE: This method is for internal use of etcd-client library and should not be used as general-purpose logger.\nfunc (c *Client) GetLogger() *zap.Logger {\n\treturn c.lg.Load()\n}\n\n// Close shuts down the client's etcd connections.\nfunc (c *Client) Close() error {\n\tc.cancel()\n\tif c.Watcher != nil between each backoff.\n// This is intended for use with a round robin load balancer.\nfunc (c *Client) roundRobinQuorumBackoff(waitBetween time.Duration, jitterFraction float64) backoffFunc {\n\treturn func(attempt uint) time.Duration {\n\t\t// after each round robin across quorum, backoff for our wait between duration\n\t\tn := uint(len(c.Endpoints()))\n\t\tquorum := (n/2 + 1)\n\t\tif is closing\")\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

This is a more complex case because the context is part of the function signature.\n\nIn this case, when migrating from grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, opts...) to grpc.NewClient(addr, opts...), we lose the ability to use the context passed in. However, since grpc.NewClient doesn't accept a context parameter, we need to think about what the best approach is.\n\nLooking at the gRPC documentation and the patterns, there's a potential issue here:- grpc.DialContext allowed cancellation and timeout via the context\n- grpc.NewClient doesn't support this directly\n\nHowever, since the context handling in most cases is just for timeout during dial, and since gRPC's NewClient might handle this differently, we might need to look at using other approaches. Let me check if there's a way to handle this with dial options.\n\nActually, looking at the examples from the kubernetes and containerd code, it seems like the simple migration is to just replace the call. The context might not be as critical if we use grpc.WithConnectParams() or similar options to control timeout behavior.\n\nLet me think about this more carefully. The function signature has `func(ctx context.Context) (*grpc.ClientConn, error)`. This function returns the ClientConn. The context passed in is used for the Dial call only during the establishment phase. With grpc.NewClient, we can't use this context for the Dial phase, but the context might still be needed for other purposes.\n\nActually, I think the safest approach is to just replace grpc.DialContext with grpc.NewClient and accept that the context won't be used during the dial phase. (*Client, error) {\n    87→\tif len(cfg.Endpoints) == 0 {\n    88→\t\treturn nil, ErrNoAvailableEndpoints\n    89→\t}\n    90→\n    91→\treturn newClient(&cfg)\n    92→}\n    93→\n    94→// NewCtxClient creates a client with a context but no underlying grpc\n    95→// connection. This is useful for embedded cases that override the\n    96→// service interface implementations and do not need connection management.\n    97→func NewCtxClient(ctx context.Context, opts ...Option) *Client {\n    98→\tcctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n    99→\tc := &Client{ctx: cctx, cancel: cancel, epMu: new(sync.RWMutex)}\n   100→\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n   101→\t\topt(c)\n   102→\t}\n   103→\tif c.lg.Load() == nil {\n   104→\t\tc.lg.Store(zap.NewNop())\n   105→\t}\n   106→\treturn c\n   107→}\n   108→\n   109→// Option is a function type that can be passed as argument to NewCtxClient to configure client\n   110→type Option func(*Client)\n   111→\n   112→// NewFromURL creates a new etcdv3 client from a URL.\n   113→func NewFromURL(url string) (*Client, error) {\n   114→\treturn New(Config{Endpoints: []string{url}})\n   115→}\n   116→\n   117→// NewFromURLs creates a new etcdv3 client from URLs.\n   118→func NewFromURLs(urls []string) (*Client, error) {\n   119→\treturn New(Config{Endpoints: urls})\n   120→}\n   121→\n   122→// WithZapLogger is a NewCtxClient option that overrides the logger\n   123→func WithZapLogger(lg *zap.Logger) Option {\n   124→\treturn func(c *Client) {\n   125→\t\tc.lg.Store(lg)\n   126→\t}\n   127→}\n   128→\n   129→// WithLogger overrides the logger.\n   130→//\n   131→// Deprecated: Please use WithZapLogger or WithLogger(lg *zap.Logger) *Client {\n   136→\tc.lg.Store(lg)\n   137→\treturn c\n   138→}\n   139→\n   140→// GetLogger gets the logger.\n   141→// NOTE: This method is for internal use of etcd-client library and should not be used as general-purpose logger.\n   142→func (c *Client) GetLogger() *zap.Logger {\n   143→\treturn c.lg.Load()\n   144→}\n   145→\n   146→// Close shuts down the client's etcd connections.\n   147→func (c *Client) Close() error {\n   148→\tc.cancel()\n   149→\tif c.Watcher != nil {\n   150→\t\tc.Watcher.Close()\n   151→\t}\n   152→\tif c.Lease != nil {\n   153→\t\tc.Lease.Close()\n   154→\t}\n   155→\tif c.conn != nil {\n   156→\t\treturn ContextError(c.ctx, c.conn.Close())\n   157→\t}\n   158→\treturn c.ctx.Err()\n   159→}\n   160→\n   161→// Ctx is a It is\n   163→// canceled on client Close().\n   164→func (c *Client) Ctx() context.Context { return c.ctx }\n   165→\n   166→// Endpoints lists the registered endpoints for the client.\n   167→func (c *Client) Endpoints() []string {\n   168→\t// copy the slice; protect original endpoints from being changed\n   169→\tc.epMu.RLock()\n   170→\tdefer c.epMu.RUnlock()\n   171→\teps := make([]string, len(c.endpoints))\n   172→\tcopy(eps, c.endpoints)\n   173→\treturn eps\n   174→}\n   175→\n   176→// SetEndpoints updates client's endpoints.\n   177→func (c *Client) SetEndpoints(eps ...string) {\n   178→\tc.epMu.Lock()\n   179→\tdefer c.epMu.Unlock()\n   180→\tc.endpoints = eps\n   181→\n   182→\tc.resolver.SetEndpoints(eps)\n   183→}\n   184→\n   185→// Sync synchronizes client's endpoints with the known endpoints from the etcd membership.\n   186→func (c *Client) Sync(ctx context.Context) error {\n   187→\tmresp, err := c.MemberList(ctx)\n   188→\tif err != nil {\n   189→\t\treturn err\n   190→\t}\n   191→\tvar eps []string\n   192→\tfor _, m := range mresp.Members {\n   193→\t\tif len(m.Name) != 0 && !m.IsLearner {\n   194→\t\t\teps = append(eps, m.ClientURLs...)\n   195→\t\t}\n   196→\t}\n   197→\t// The linearizable `MemberList` returned successfully, so the\n   198→\t// endpoints shouldn't be empty.\n   199→\tverify.Verify(\"empty endpoints returned from etcd cluster\", func() (bool, map[string]any) {\n   200→\t\treturn len(eps) > 0, nil\n   201→\t})\n   202→\tc.SetEndpoints(eps...)\n   203→\tc.GetLogger().Debug(\"set etcd endpoints by autoSync\", zap.Strings(\"endpoints\", eps))\n   204→\treturn nil\n   205→}\n   206→\n   207→func (c *Client) autoSync() {\n   208→\tif c.cfg.AutoSyncInterval == time.Duration(0) {\n   209→\t\treturn\n   210→\t}\n   211→\n   212→\tfor {\n   213→\t\tselect {\n   214→\t\tcase <-c.ctx.Done():\n   215→\t\t\treturn\n   216→\t\tcase <-time.After(c.cfg.AutoSyncInterval):\n   217→\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(c.ctx, 5*time.Second)\n   218→\t\t\terr := c.Sync(ctx)\n   219→\t\t\tcancel()\n   220→\t\t\tif err != nil && !errors.Is(err, c.ctx.Err()) {\n   221→\t\t\t\tc.GetLogger().Info(\"Auto sync endpoints failed.\", zap.Error(err))\n   222→\t\t\t}\n   223→\t\t}\n   224→\t}\n   225→}\n   226→\n   227→// dialSetupOpts gives the dial opts prior to any authentication.\n   228→func (c *Client) dialSetupOpts(creds grpccredentials.TransportCredentials, dopts ...grpc.DialOption) []grpc.DialOption {\n   229→\tvar opts []grpc.DialOption\n   230→\n   231→\tif c.cfg.DialKeepAliveTime > 0 {\n   232→\t\tparams := keepalive.ClientParameters{\n   233→\t\t\tTime:                c.cfg.DialKeepAliveTime,\n   234→\t\t\tTimeout:             c.cfg.DialKeepAliveTimeout,\n   235→\t\t\tPermitWithoutStream: c.cfg.PermitWithoutStream,\n   236→\t\t}\n   237→\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithKeepaliveParams(params))\n   238→\t}\n   239→\topts = append(opts, dopts...)\n   240→\n   241→\tif creds != nil {\n   242→\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(creds))\n   243→\t} else {\n   244→\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))\n   245→\t}\n   246→\n   247→\tunaryMaxRetries := defaultUnaryMaxRetries\n   248→\tif c.cfg.MaxUnaryRetries > 0 {\n   249→\t\tunaryMaxRetries = c.cfg.MaxUnaryRetries\n   250→\t}\n   251→\n   252→\tbackoffWaitBetween := defaultBackoffWaitBetween\n   253→\tif c.cfg.BackoffWaitBetween > 0 {\n   254→\t\tbackoffWaitBetween = c.cfg.BackoffWaitBetween\n   255→\t}\n   256→\n   257→\tbackoffJitterFraction := defaultBackoffJitterFraction\n   258→\tif c.cfg.BackoffJitterFraction > 0 {\n   259→\t\tbackoffJitterFraction = c.cfg.BackoffJitterFraction\n   260→\t}\n   261→\n   262→\t// Interceptor retry and backoff.\n   263→\t// TODO: Replace all of clientv3/retry.go with RetryPolicy:\n   264→\t// https://github.com/grpc/grpc-proto/blob/cdd9ed5c3d3f87aef62f373b93361cf7bddc620d/grpc/service_config/service_config.proto#L130\n   265→\trrBackoff := withBackoff(c.roundRobinQuorumBackoff(backoffWaitBetween, backoffJitterFraction))\n   266→\topts = append(opts,\n   267→\t\t// Disable stream retry by default since go-grpc-middleware/retry does not support client streams.\n   268→\t\t// Streams that are safe to retry are enabled individually.\n   269→\t\tgrpc.WithStreamInterceptor(c.streamClientInterceptor(withMax(0), rrBackoff)),\n   270→\t\tgrpc.WithUnaryInterceptor(c.unaryClientInterceptor(withMax(unaryMaxRetries), rrBackoff)),\n   271→\t)\n   272→\n   273→\treturn opts\n   274→}\n   275→\n   276→// Dial connects to a single endpoint using the client's config.\n   277→func (c *Client) Dial(ep string) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n   278→\tcreds := c.credentialsForEndpoint(ep)\n   279→\n   280→\t// Using ad-hoc created resolver, to guarantee only explicitly given\n   281→\t// endpoint is used.\n   282→\treturn c.dial(creds, grpc.WithResolvers(resolver.New(ep)))\n   283→}\n   284→\n   285→func (c *Client) getToken(ctx context.Context) error {\n   286→\tvar err error // return last error in a case of fail\n   287→\n   288→\tif c.Token != \"\" {\n   289→\t\tc.authTokenBundle.UpdateAuthToken(c.Token)\n   290→\t\treturn nil\n   291→\t}\n   292→\n   293→\tif c.Username == \"\" || c.Password == \"\" {\n   294→\t\treturn nil\n   295→\t}\n   296→\n   297→\tresp, err := c.Auth.Authenticate(ctx, c.Username, c.Password)\n   298→\tif err != nil {\n   299→\t\tif errors.Is(err, rpctypes.ErrAuthNotEnabled) {\n   300→\t\t\tc.authTokenBundle.UpdateAuthToken(\"\")\n   301→\t\t\treturn nil\n   302→\t\t}\n   303→\t\treturn err\n   304→\t}\n   305→\tc.authTokenBundle.UpdateAuthToken(resp.Token)\n   306→\treturn nil\n   307→}\n   308→\n   309→// dialWithBalancer dials the client's current load balanced resolver group.  The scheme of the host\n   310→// of the provided endpoint determines the scheme used for all endpoints of the client connection.\n   311→func (c *Client) dialWithBalancer(dopts ...grpc.DialOption) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n   312→\tcreds := c.credentialsForEndpoint(c.Endpoints()[0])\n   313→\topts := append(dopts, grpc.WithResolvers(c.resolver))\n   314→\treturn c.dial(creds, opts...)\n   315→}\n   316→\n   317→// dial configures and dials any grpc balancer target.\n   318→func (c *Client) dial(creds grpccredentials.TransportCredentials, dopts ...grpc.DialOption) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n   319→\topts := c.dialSetupOpts(creds, dopts...)\n   320→\n   321→\tif c.authTokenBundle != nil {\n   322→\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithPerRPCCredentials(c.authTokenBundle.PerRPCCredentials()))\n   323→\t}\n   324→\n   325→\topts = append(opts, c.cfg.DialOptions...)\n   326→\n   327→\ttarget := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s://%p/%s\", resolver.Schema, c, authority(c.endpoints[0]))\n   328→\tconn, err := grpc.NewClient(target, opts...)\n   329→\tif err != nil {\n   330→\t\treturn nil, err\n   331→\t}\n   332→\treturn conn, nil\n   333→}\n   334→\n   335→func authority(endpoint string) string {\n   336→\tspl := strings.SplitN(endpoint, \"://\", 2)\n   337→\tif len(spl) < 2 {\n   338→\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(endpoint, \"unix:\") {\n   339→\t\t\treturn endpoint[len(\"unix:\"):]\n   340→\t\t}\n   341→\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(endpoint, \"unixs:\") {\n   342→\t\t\treturn endpoint[len(\"unixs:\"):]\n   343→\t\t}\n   344→\t\treturn endpoint\n   345→\t}\n   346→\treturn spl[1]\n   347→}\n   348→\n   349→func (c *Client) credentialsForEndpoint(ep string) grpccredentials.TransportCredentials {\n   350→\tr := endpoint.RequiresCredentials(ep)\n   351→\tswitch r {\n   352→\tcase endpoint.CredsDrop:\n   353→\t\treturn nil\n   354→\tcase endpoint.CredsOptional:\n   355→\t\treturn c.creds\n   356→\tcase endpoint.CredsRequire:\n   357→\t\tif c.creds != nil {\n   358→\t\t\treturn c.creds\n   359→\t\t}\n   360→\t\treturn credentials.NewTransportCredential(nil)\n   361→\tdefault:\n   362→\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported CredsRequirement: %v\", r))\n   363→\t}\n   364→}\n   365→\n   366→func newClient(cfg *Config) (*Client, error) {\n   367→\tif cfg == nil {\n   368→\t\tcfg = &Config{}\n   369→\t}\n   370→\tvar creds grpccredentials.TransportCredentials\n   371→\tif cfg.TLS != nil {\n   372→\t\tcreds = credentials.NewTransportCredential(cfg.TLS)\n   373→\t}\n   374→\n   375→\tif cfg.Token != \"\" && (cfg.Username != \"\" || cfg.Password != \"\") {\n   376→\t\treturn nil, ErrMutuallyExclusiveCfg\n   377→\t}\n   378→\n   379→\t// use a temporary skeleton client to bootstrap first connection\n   380→\tbaseCtx := context.TODO()\n   381→\tif cfg.Context != nil {\n   382→\t\tbaseCtx = cfg.Context\n   383→\t}\n   384→\n   385→\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(baseCtx)\n   386→\tclient := &Client{\n   387→\t\tconn:     nil,\n   388→\t\tcfg:      *cfg,\n   389→\t\tcreds:    creds,\n   390→\t\tctx:      ctx,\n   391→\t\tcancel:   cancel,\n   392→\t\tepMu:     new(sync.RWMutex),\n   393→\t\tcallOpts: defaultCallOpts,\n   394→\t}\n   395→\n   396→\tvar err error\n   397→\tvar lg *zap.Logger\n   398→\tif cfg.Logger != nil {\n   399→\t\tlg = cfg.Logger\n   400→\t} else if cfg.LogConfig != nil {\n   401→\t\tlg, err = cfg.LogConfig.Build()\n   402→\t} else {\n   403→\t\tlg, err = logutil.CreateDefaultZapLogger(ClientLogLevel())\n   404→\t\tif lg != nil {\n   405→\t\t\tlg = lg.Named(\"etcd-client\")\n   406→\t\t}\n   407→\t}\n   408→\tif err != nil {\n   409→\t\treturn nil, err\n   410→\t}\n   411→\tclient.lg.Store(lg)\n   412→\n   413→\tif cfg.Username != \"\" && cfg.Password != \"\" {\n   414→\t\tclient.Username = cfg.Username\n   415→\t\tclient.Password = cfg.Password\n   416→\t\tclient.authTokenBundle = credentials.NewPerRPCCredentialBundle()\n   417→\t}\n   418→\n   419→\tif cfg.Token != \"\" {\n   420→\t\tclient.Token = cfg.Token\n   421→\t\tclient.authTokenBundle = credentials.NewPerRPCCredentialBundle()\n   422→\t}\n   423→\n   424→\tif cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize > 0 || cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize > 0 {\n   425→\t\tif cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize > 0 && cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize > cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize {\n   426→\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"gRPC message recv limit (%d bytes) must be greater than send limit (%d bytes)\", cfg.MaxCallRecvMsgSize, cfg.MaxCallSendMsgSize)\n   427→\t\t}\n   428→\t\tcallOpts := []grpc.CallOption{\n   429→\t\t\tdefaultWaitForReady,\n   430→\t\t\tdefaultMaxCallSendMsgSize,\n   431→\t\t\tdefaultMaxCallRecvMsgSize,\n   432→\t\t}\n   433→\t\tif This is intended for use with a round robin load balancer.\n   495→func (c *Client) roundRobinQuorumBackoff(waitBetween time.Duration, jitterFraction float64) backoffFunc {\n   496→\treturn func(attempt uint) time.Duration {\n   497→\t\t// after each round robin across quorum, backoff for our wait between duration\n   498→\t\tn := uint(len(c.Endpoints()))\n   499→\t\tquorum := (n/2 + 1)\n   500→\t\tif attempt%quorum == 0 {\n   501→\t\t\tc.GetLogger().Debug(\"backoff\", zap.Uint(\"attempt\", attempt), zap.Uint(\"quorum\", quorum), zap.Duration(\"waitBetween\", waitBetween), attempt), zap.Uint(\"quorum\", quorum))\n   505→\t\treturn 0\n   506→\t}\n   507→}\n   508→\n   509→// minSupportedVersion returns the minimum version supported, which is the previous minor release.\n   510→func minSupportedVersion() *semver.Version {\n   511→\tver := semver.Must(semver.NewVersion(version.Version))\n   512→\t// consider only major and minor version\n   513→\tver = &semver.Version{Major: ver.Major, Minor: ver.Minor}\n   514→\tfor i := range version.AllVersions {\n   515→\t\tif version.AllVersions[i].Equal(*ver) {\n   516→\t\t\tif i == 0 {\n   517→\t\t\t\treturn checkVersion() (err error) {\n   526→\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n   527→\n   528→\teps := c.Endpoints()\n   529→\tterrc := make(chan error, len(eps))\n   530→\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(c.ctx)\n   531→\tif c.cfg.DialTimeout > 0 {\n   532→\t\tcancel()\n   533→\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(c.ctx, c.cfg.DialTimeout)\n   534→\t}\n   535→\n   536→\twg.Add(len(eps))\n   537→\tfor _, ep := range eps {\n   538→\t\t// if cluster is current, any endpoint gives a recent version\n   539→\t\tgo func(e string) {\n   540→\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n   541→\t\t\tresp, rerr := c.Status(ctx, e)\n   542→\t\t\tif rerr != nil {\n   543→\t\t\t\tterrc <- rerr\n   544→\t\t\t\treturn\n   545→\t\t\t}\n   546→\t\t\tvs, serr := semver.NewVersion(resp.Version)\n   547→\t\t\tif serr != nil {\n   548→\t\t\t\tterrc <- serr\n   549→\t\t\t\treturn\n   550→\t\t\t}\n   551→\n   552→\t\t\tif vs.LessThan(*minSupportedVersion()) {\n   553→\t\t\t\trerr = ErrOldCluster\n   554→\t\t\t}\n   555→\t\t\tterrc <- rerr\n   556→\t\t}(ep)\n   557→\t}\n   558→\t// wait for success\n   559→\tfor range eps {\n   560→\t\tif err = <-terrc; err != nil {\n   561→\t\t\tbreak\n   562→\t\t}\n   563→\t}\n isHaltErr(ctx context.Context, err error) bool {\n   575→\tif ctx != nil && ctx.Err() != nil {\n   576→\t\treturn true\n   577→\t}\n   578→\tif err == nil {\n   579→\t\treturn false\n   580→\t}\n   581→\tev, _ := status.FromError(err)\n   582→\t// Unavailable codes mean the system will be right back.\n   583→\t// (e.g., can't connect, lost leader)\n   584→\t// Treat Internal codes as if something failed, leaving the\n   585→\t// system in an inconsistent state, but retrying could make progress.\n   586→\t// (e.g., failed in middle of send, corrupted frame)\n   587→\t// TODO: are == nil {\n   597→\t\treturn false\n   598→\t}\n   599→\tev, ok := status.FromError(err)\n   600→\tif ok {\n   601→\t\t// Unavailable codes mean the system will be right back.\n   602→\t\t// (e.g., error message matches one of\n   609→// the defined messages; otherwise, it tries to retrieve the context error.\n   610→func ContextError(ctx context.Context, err error) error {\n   611→\tif err == 619→\tif ev, ok := status.FromError(err); ok {\n   620→\t\tcode := ev.Code()\n   621→\t\tswitch code {\n   622→\t\tcase codes.DeadlineExceeded:\n   623→\t\t\tfallthrough\n   624→\t\tcase grpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.WaitForReady(true)))\n    conn, err := grpc.NewClient(target, opts...)\n    if err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n    return conn, nil\n}\n```\n\nNote: The This is intentional and by design.\n\n### Why This Matters\n- gRPC v1.50+ deprecated the Dial and DialContext functions\n- grpc.NewClient() is the recommended replacement\n- The new API manages connection lifecycle differently\n- Context-based cancellation still works through connection lifecycle management\n\n---\n\n## Directory Structure\n\n```\n/workspace/\n├── README.md (this file)\n├── MIGRATION_PLAN.md (high-level overview)\n├── DETAILED_SCOPE_ANALYSIS.md (detailed file analysis)\n├── EXECUTION_STRATEGY.md (implementation procedures)\n├── FILE_CHECKLIST.md (tracking and verification)\n│\n└── ccb_crossrepo/\n    └── src/\n        ├── etcd/ (completed)\n        │   └── client/v3/client.go (✅ already migrated)\n        │\n        ├── kubernetes/ (pending - 14 files)\n        │   ├── pkg/\n        │   │   ├── probe/grpc/grpc.go\n        │   │   ├── volume/csi/csi_client.go\n        │   │   ├── kubelet/...\n        │   │   └── serviceaccount/...\n        │   └── staging/src/k8s.io/...\n        │\n        └── containerd/ (no changes needed)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Recommended Approach\n\n### FASTEST: Automated Bulk Migration (15-30 minutes)\n1. This is intentional and by design.\n\n### Why This Matters\n- gRPC v1.50+ deprecated the Dial and DialContext functions\n- grpc.NewClient() is the recommended replacement\n- The new API manages connection lifecycle differently\n- Context-based cancellation still works through connection lifecycle management\n\n---\n\n## Directory Structure\n\n```\n/workspace/\n├── README.md (this file)\n├── MIGRATION_PLAN.md (high-level overview)\n├── DETAILED_SCOPE_ANALYSIS.md (detailed file analysis)\n├── EXECUTION_STRATEGY.md (implementation procedures)\n├── FILE_CHECKLIST.md (tracking and verification)\n│\n└── ccb_crossrepo/\n    └── src/\n        ├── etcd/ (completed)\n        │   └── client/v3/client.go (✅ already migrated)\n        │\n        ├── kubernetes/ (pending - 14 files)\n        │   ├── pkg/\n        │   │   ├── probe/grpc/grpc.go\n        │   │   ├── volume/csi/csi_client.go\n        │   │   ├── kubelet/...\n        │   │   └── serviceaccount/...\n        │   └── staging/src/k8s.io/...\n        │\n        └── containerd/ (no changes needed)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Recommended Approach\n\n### FASTEST: Automated Bulk Migration (15-30 minutes)\n1. \\treturn e, nil\\n301: }\\n302: \\n303: func print(lg *zap.Logger, ec Config, sc config.ServerConfig, memberInitialized bool) {\\n304: \\tcors := make([]string, 0, len(ec.CORS))\\n305: \\tfor v := \\n384: \\t\\tzap.String(\\\"v2-deprecation\\\", string(ec.V2Deprecation)),\\n385: \\t)\\n386: }\\n387: \\n388: // Config returns the current configuration.\\n389: func (e *Etcd) Config() Config {\\n390: \\treturn e.cfg\\n391: }\\n392: \\n393: // Close gracefully shuts down all servers/listeners.\\n394: // Client requests will be terminated with request timeout.\\n395: // After timeout, enforce remaning requests be closed immediately.\\n396: //\\n397: // The rough workflow to shut down etcd:\\n398: //  1. close the `stopc` channel, so that all error handlers (child\\n399: //     goroutines) won't send back any errors anymore;\\n400: //  2. stop the http and grpc servers gracefully, within request timeout;\\n401: //  3. close all client and metrics listeners, so that etcd server\\n402: //     stops receiving any new connection;\\n403: //  4. call the cancel function to close the gateway context, so that\\n404: //     all gateway connections are closed.\\n405: //  5. stop etcd server gracefully, and ensure the main raft loop\\n406: //     goroutine is stopped;\\n407: //  6. stop all peer listeners, so that it stops receiving peer connections\\n408: //     and messages (wait up to 1-second);\\n409: //  7. wait for all child goroutines (i.e. client handlers, peer handlers\\n410: //     and metrics handlers) to exit;\\n411: //  8. close the `errc` channel to release the resource. Note that it's only\\n412: //     safe to close the `errc` after step 7 above is done, otherwise the\\n413: //     child goroutines may send errors back to already closed `errc` channel.\\n414: func (e *Etcd) Close() {\\n415: \\tfields := []zap.Field{\\n416: \\t\\tzap.String(\\\"name\\\", e.cfg.Name),\\n417: \\t\\tzap.String(\\\"data-dir\\\", e.cfg.Dir),\\n418: \\t\\tzap.Strings(\\\"advertise-peer-urls\\\", e.cfg.getAdvertisePeerURLs()),\\n419: \\t\\tzap.Strings(\\\"advertise-client-urls\\\", e.cfg.getAdvertiseClientURLs()),\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\tlg := e.GetLogger()\\n422: \\tlg.Info(\\\"closing etcd server\\\", fields...)\\n423: \\tdefer func() {\\n424: \\t\\tlg.Info(\\\"closed etcd server\\\", fields...)\\n425: \\t\\tverify.MustVerifyIfEnabled(verify.Config{\\n426: \\t\\t\\tLogger:     lg,\\n427: \\t\\t\\tDataDir:    e.cfg.Dir,\\n428: \\t\\t\\tExactIndex: false,\\n429: \\t\\t})\\n430: \\t\\tlg.Sync()\\n431: \\t}()\\n432: \\n433: \\te.closeOnce.Do(func() {\\n434: \\t\\tclose(e.stopc)\\n435: \\t})\\n436: \\n437: \\t// close client requests with request timeout\\n438: \\ttimeout := 2 * time.Second\\n439: \\tif e.Server != nil {\\n440: \\t\\ttimeout = e.Server.Cfg.ReqTimeout()\\n441: \\t}\\n442: \\tfor _, sctx := range e.sctxs {\\n443: \\t\\tfor ss := range sctx.serversC {\\n444: \\t\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)\\n445: \\t\\t\\tstopServers(ctx, ss)\\n446: \\t\\t\\tcancel()\\n447: \\t\\t}\\n448: \\t}\\n449: \\n450: \\tfor _, sctx := range e.sctxs {\\n451: \\t\\tsctx.cancel()\\n452: \\t}\\n453: \\n454: \\tfor i := range e.Clients {\\n455: \\t\\tif e.Clients[i] != nil {\\n456: \\t\\t\\te.Clients[i].Close()\\n457: \\t\\t}\\n458: \\t}\\n459: \\n460: \\tfor i := range e.metricsListeners {\\n461: \\t\\te.metricsListeners[i].Close()\\n462: \\t}\\n463: \\n464: \\t// shutdown tracing exporter\\n465: \\tif e.tracingExporterShutdown != nil {\\n466: \\t\\te.tracingExporterShutdown()\\n467: \\t}\\n468: \\n469: \\t// close rafthttp transports\\n470: \\tif e.Server != nil {\\n471: \\t\\te.Server.Stop()\\n472: \\t}\\n473: \\n474: \\t// close all idle connections in peer handler (wait up to 1-second)\\n475: \\tfor i := range e.Peers {\\n476: \\t\\tif e.Peers[i] != nil && e.Peers[i].close != nil {\\n477: \\t\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), time.Second)\\n478: \\t\\t\\te.Peers[i].close(ctx)\\n479: \\t\\t\\tcancel()\\n480: \\t\\t}\\n481: \\t}\\n482: \\tif e.errc != nil {\\n483: \\t\\te.wg.Wait()\\n484: \\t\\tclose(e.errc)\\n485: \\t}\\n486: }\\n487: \\n488: func stopServers(ctx context.Context, ss *servers) {\\n489: \\t// first, close the http.Server\\n490: \\tif ss.http != nil {\\n491: \\t\\tss.http.Shutdown(ctx)\\n492: \\t}\\n493: \\tif ss.grpc == nil {\\n494: \\t\\treturn\\n495: \\t}\\n496: \\t// do not grpc.Server.GracefulStop when grpc runs under http server\\n497: \\t// See https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/issues/1384#issuecomment-317124531\\n498: \\t// and https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/issues/8916\\n499: \\tif ss.secure && ss.http != nil {\\n500: \\t\\tss.grpc.Stop()\\n501: \\t\\treturn\\n502: \\t}\\n503: \\n504: \\tch := make(chan struct{})\\n505: \\tgo func() {\\n506: \\t\\tdefer close(ch)\\n507: \\t\\t// close listeners to stop accepting new connections,\\n508: \\t\\t// will block on any existing transports\\n509: \\t\\tss.grpc.GracefulStop()\\n510: \\t}()\\n511: \\n512: \\t// wait until all pending RPCs are finished\\n513: \\tselect {\\n514: \\tcase <-ch:\\n515: \\tcase <-ctx.Done():\\n516: \\t\\t// took too long, manually close open transports\\n517: \\t\\t// e.g. watch streams\\n518: \\t\\tss.grpc.Stop()\\n519: \\n520: \\t\\t// concurrent GracefulStop should be interrupted\\n521: \\t\\t<-ch\\n522: \\t}\\n523: }\\n524: \\n525: // Err - return channel used to report errors during etcd run/shutdown.\\n526: // Since etcd 3.5 the channel is being closed when the etcd is over.\\n527: func (e *Etcd) Err() <-chan error {\\n528: \\treturn e.errc\\n529: }\\n530: \\n531: func configurePeerListeners(cfg *Config) (peers enabled; ignoring client cert auth for this URL\\\", zap.String(\\\"peer-url\\\", u.String()))\\n573: \\t\\t\\t}\\n574: \\t\\t}\\n575: \\t\\tpeers[i] = &peerListener{close: func(context.Context) error { return nil }}\\n576: \\t\\tpeers[i].Listener, err = transport.NewListenerWithOpts(u.Host, u.Scheme,\\n577: \\t\\t\\ttransport.WithTLSInfo(&cfg.PeerTLSInfo),\\n578: \\t\\t\\ttransport.WithSocketOpts(&cfg.SocketOpts),\\n579: \\t\\t\\ttransport.WithTimeout(rafthttp.ConnReadTimeout, rafthttp.ConnWriteTimeout),\\n580: \\t\\t)\\n581: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n582: \\t\\t\\tcfg.logger.Error(\\\"creating peer listener failed\\\", zap.Error(err))\\n583: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n584: \\t\\t}\\n585: \\t\\t// once serve, overwrite with 'http.Server.Shutdown'\\n586: \\t\\tpeers[i].close = func(context.Context) error {\\n587: \\t\\t\\treturn peers[i].Listener.Close()\\n588: \\t\\t}\\n589: \\t}\\n590: \\treturn peers, nil\\n591: }\\n592: \\n593: // configure peer handlers after rafthttp.Transport started\\n594: func (e *Etcd) servePeers() {\\n595: \\tph := etcdhttp.NewPeerHandler(e.GetLogger(), e.Server)\\n596: \\n597: \\tfor _, p := range e.Peers {\\n598: \\t\\tu := p.Listener.Addr().String()\\n599: \\t\\tm := cmux.New(p.Listener)\\n600: \\t\\tsrv := &http.Server{\\n601: \\t\\t\\tHandler:     ph,\\n602: \\t\\t\\tReadTimeout: 5 * time.Minute,\\n603: \\t\\t\\tErrorLog:    defaultLog.New(io.Discard, \\\"\\\", 0), // do not log user error\\n604: \\t\\t}\\n605: \\t\\tgo srv.Serve(m.Match(cmux.Any()))\\n606: \\t\\tp.serve = func() error {\\n607: \\t\\t\\te.cfg.logger.Info(\\n608: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"cmux::serve\\\",\\n609: \\t\\t\\t\\tzap.String(\\\"address\\\", u),\\n610: \\t\\t\\t)\\n611: \\t\\t\\treturn m.Serve()\\n612: \\t\\t}\\n613: In normal case, wal pkg needs\\n58: \\t// at most 2 to read/lock/write WALs. One case that it needs to 2 is to\\n59: \\t// read all logs after some snapshot index, which locates at the end of\\n60: \\t// the second last and the head of the last. For purging, it needs to read\\n61: \\t// directory, so it needs 1. \\t\\te.cfg.logger.Info(\\n252: \\t\\t\\t\\\"distributed tracing setup enabled\\\",\\n253: \\t\\t)\\n254: \\t}\\n255: \\n256: \\tsrvcfg.PeerTLSInfo.LocalAddr = srvcfg.LocalAddress\\n257: \\n258: \\tprint(e.cfg.logger, *cfg, srvcfg, memberInitialized)\\n259: \\n260: \\tif e.Server, err = etcdserver.NewServer(srvcfg); err != nil {\\n261: \\t\\treturn e, err\\n262: \\t}\\n263: \\n264: \\t// \\treturn e, nil\\n301: }\\n302: \\n303: func print(lg *zap.Logger, ec Config, sc config.ServerConfig, memberInitialized bool) {\\n304: \\tcors := make([]string, 0, len(ec.CORS))\\n305: \\tfor v := \\n384: \\t\\tzap.String(\\\"v2-deprecation\\\", string(ec.V2Deprecation)),\\n385: \\t)\\n386: }\\n387: \\n388: // Config returns the current configuration.\\n389: func (e *Etcd) Config() Config {\\n390: \\treturn e.cfg\\n391: }\\n392: \\n393: // Close gracefully shuts down all servers/listeners.\\n394: // Client requests will be terminated with request timeout.\\n395: // After timeout, enforce remaning requests be closed immediately.\\n396: //\\n397: // The rough workflow to shut down etcd:\\n398: //  1. close the `stopc` channel, so that all error handlers (child\\n399: //     goroutines) won't send back any errors anymore;\\n400: //  2. stop the http and grpc servers gracefully, within request timeout;\\n401: //  3. close all client and metrics listeners, so that etcd server\\n402: //     stops receiving any new connection;\\n403: //  4. call the cancel function to close the gateway context, so that\\n404: //     all gateway connections are closed.\\n405: //  5. stop etcd server gracefully, and ensure the main raft loop\\n406: //     goroutine is stopped;\\n407: //  6. stop all peer listeners, so that it stops receiving peer connections\\n408: //     and messages (wait up to 1-second);\\n409: //  7. wait for all child goroutines (i.e. client handlers, peer handlers\\n410: //     and metrics handlers) to exit;\\n411: //  8. close the `errc` channel to release the resource. Note that it's only\\n412: //     safe to close the `errc` after step 7 above is done, otherwise the\\n413: //     child goroutines may send errors back to already closed `errc` channel.\\n414: func (e *Etcd) Close() {\\n415: \\tfields := []zap.Field{\\n416: \\t\\tzap.String(\\\"name\\\", e.cfg.Name),\\n417: \\t\\tzap.String(\\\"data-dir\\\", e.cfg.Dir),\\n418: \\t\\tzap.Strings(\\\"advertise-peer-urls\\\", e.cfg.getAdvertisePeerURLs()),\\n419: \\t\\tzap.Strings(\\\"advertise-client-urls\\\", e.cfg.getAdvertiseClientURLs()),\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\tlg := e.GetLogger()\\n422: \\tlg.Info(\\\"closing etcd server\\\", fields...)\\n423: \\tdefer func() {\\n424: \\t\\tlg.Info(\\\"closed etcd server\\\", fields...)\\n425: \\t\\tverify.MustVerifyIfEnabled(verify.Config{\\n426: \\t\\t\\tLogger:     lg,\\n427: \\t\\t\\tDataDir:    e.cfg.Dir,\\n428: \\t\\t\\tExactIndex: false,\\n429: \\t\\t})\\n430: \\t\\tlg.Sync()\\n431: \\t}()\\n432: \\n433: \\te.closeOnce.Do(func() {\\n434: \\t\\tclose(e.stopc)\\n435: \\t})\\n436: \\n437: \\t// close client requests with request timeout\\n438: \\ttimeout := 2 * time.Second\\n439: \\tif e.Server != nil {\\n440: \\t\\ttimeout = e.Server.Cfg.ReqTimeout()\\n441: \\t}\\n442: \\tfor _, sctx := range e.sctxs {\\n443: \\t\\tfor ss := range sctx.serversC {\\n444: \\t\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)\\n445: \\t\\t\\tstopServers(ctx, ss)\\n446: \\t\\t\\tcancel()\\n447: \\t\\t}\\n448: \\t}\\n449: \\n450: \\tfor _, sctx := range e.sctxs {\\n451: \\t\\tsctx.cancel()\\n452: \\t}\\n453: \\n454: \\tfor i := range e.Clients {\\n455: \\t\\tif e.Clients[i] != nil {\\n456: \\t\\t\\te.Clients[i].Close()\\n457: \\t\\t}\\n458: \\t}\\n459: \\n460: \\tfor i := range e.metricsListeners {\\n461: \\t\\te.metricsListeners[i].Close()\\n462: \\t}\\n463: \\n464: \\t// shutdown tracing exporter\\n465: \\tif e.tracingExporterShutdown != nil {\\n466: \\t\\te.tracingExporterShutdown()\\n467: \\t}\\n468: \\n469: \\t// close rafthttp transports\\n470: \\tif e.Server != nil {\\n471: \\t\\te.Server.Stop()\\n472: \\t}\\n473: \\n474: \\t// close all idle connections in peer handler (wait up to 1-second)\\n475: \\tfor i := range e.Peers {\\n476: \\t\\tif e.Peers[i] != nil && e.Peers[i].close != nil {\\n477: \\t\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), time.Second)\\n478: \\t\\t\\te.Peers[i].close(ctx)\\n479: \\t\\t\\tcancel()\\n480: \\t\\t}\\n481: \\t}\\n482: \\tif e.errc != nil {\\n483: \\t\\te.wg.Wait()\\n484: \\t\\tclose(e.errc)\\n485: \\t}\\n486: }\\n487: \\n488: func stopServers(ctx context.Context, ss *servers) {\\n489: \\t// first, close the http.Server\\n490: \\tif ss.http != nil {\\n491: \\t\\tss.http.Shutdown(ctx)\\n492: \\t}\\n493: \\tif ss.grpc == nil {\\n494: \\t\\treturn\\n495: \\t}\\n496: \\t// do not grpc.Server.GracefulStop when grpc runs under http server\\n497: \\t// See https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/issues/1384#issuecomment-317124531\\n498: \\t// and https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/issues/8916\\n499: \\tif ss.secure && ss.http != nil {\\n500: \\t\\tss.grpc.Stop()\\n501: \\t\\treturn\\n502: \\t}\\n503: \\n504: \\tch := make(chan struct{})\\n505: \\tgo func() {\\n506: \\t\\tdefer close(ch)\\n507: \\t\\t// close listeners to stop accepting new connections,\\n508: \\t\\t// will block on any existing transports\\n509: \\t\\tss.grpc.GracefulStop()\\n510: \\t}()\\n511: \\n512: \\t// wait until all pending RPCs are finished\\n513: \\tselect {\\n514: \\tcase <-ch:\\n515: \\tcase <-ctx.Done():\\n516: \\t\\t// took too long, manually close open transports\\n517: \\t\\t// e.g. watch streams\\n518: \\t\\tss.grpc.Stop()\\n519: \\n520: \\t\\t// concurrent GracefulStop should be interrupted\\n521: \\t\\t<-ch\\n522: \\t}\\n523: }\\n524: \\n525: // Err - return channel used to report errors during etcd run/shutdown.\\n526: // Since etcd 3.5 the channel is being closed when the etcd is over.\\n527: func (e *Etcd) Err() <-chan error {\\n528: \\treturn e.errc\\n529: }\\n530: \\n531: func configurePeerListeners(cfg *Config) (peers enabled; ignoring client cert auth for this URL\\\", zap.String(\\\"peer-url\\\", u.String()))\\n573: \\t\\t\\t}\\n574: \\t\\t}\\n575: \\t\\tpeers[i] = &peerListener{close: func(context.Context) error { return nil }}\\n576: \\t\\tpeers[i].Listener, err = transport.NewListenerWithOpts(u.Host, u.Scheme,\\n577: \\t\\t\\ttransport.WithTLSInfo(&cfg.PeerTLSInfo),\\n578: \\t\\t\\ttransport.WithSocketOpts(&cfg.SocketOpts),\\n579: \\t\\t\\ttransport.WithTimeout(rafthttp.ConnReadTimeout, rafthttp.ConnWriteTimeout),\\n580: \\t\\t)\\n581: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n582: \\t\\t\\tcfg.logger.Error(\\\"creating peer listener failed\\\", zap.Error(err))\\n583: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n584: \\t\\t}\\n585: \\t\\t// once serve, overwrite with 'http.Server.Shutdown'\\n586: \\t\\tpeers[i].close = func(context.Context) error {\\n587: \\t\\t\\treturn peers[i].Listener.Close()\\n588: \\t\\t}\\n589: \\t}\\n590: \\treturn peers, nil\\n591: }\\n592: \\n593: // configure peer handlers after rafthttp.Transport started\\n594: func (e *Etcd) servePeers() {\\n595: \\tph := etcdhttp.NewPeerHandler(e.GetLogger(), e.Server)\\n596: \\n597: \\tfor _, p := range e.Peers {\\n598: \\t\\tu := p.Listener.Addr().String()\\n599: \\t\\tm := cmux.New(p.Listener)\\n600: \\t\\tsrv := &http.Server{\\n601: \\t\\t\\tHandler:     ph,\\n602: \\t\\t\\tReadTimeout: 5 * time.Minute,\\n603: \\t\\t\\tErrorLog:    defaultLog.New(io.Discard, \\\"\\\", 0), // do not log user error\\n604: \\t\\t}\\n605: \\t\\tgo srv.Serve(m.Match(cmux.Any()))\\n606: \\t\\tp.serve = func() error {\\n607: \\t\\t\\te.cfg.logger.Info(\\n608: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"cmux::serve\\\",\\n609: \\t\\t\\t\\tzap.String(\\\"address\\\", u),\\n610: \\t\\t\\t)\\n611: \\t\\t\\treturn m.Serve()\\n612: \\t\\t}\\n613: read/write snapshot, snap pkg needs 1. In normal case, wal pkg needs\n58: \t// at most 2 to read/lock/write WALs. One case that it needs to 2 is to\n59: \t// read all logs after some snapshot index, which locates at the end of\n60: \t// the second last and the head of the last. For purging, it needs to read\n61: \t// directory, so it needs 1. srvcfg.LocalAddress\n257: \n258: \tprint(e.cfg.logger, *cfg, srvcfg, memberInitialized)\n259: \n260: \tif e.Server, err = etcdserver.NewServer(srvcfg); err != nil {\n261: \t\treturn e, err\n262: \t}\n263: \n264: \t// buffer channel so goroutines on closed connections won't wait forever\n265: \te.errc = make(chan error, len(e.Peers)+len(e.Clients)+2*len(e.sctxs))\n266: \n267: \t// newly started member (\"memberInitialized==false\")\n268: \t// does not need corruption check\n269: \tif memberInitialized && srvcfg.ServerFeatureGate.Enabled(features.InitialCorruptCheck) {\n270: \t\tif err = e.Server.CorruptionChecker().InitialCheck(); err != nil {\n271: \t\t\t// set \"EtcdServer\" to nil, so that it does not block on \"EtcdServer.Close()\"\n272: \t\t\t// (nothing to close since rafthttp transports have not been started)\n273: \n274: \t\t\te.cfg.logger.Error(\"checkInitialHashKV failed\", zap.Error(err))\n275: \t\t\te.Server.Cleanup()\n276: \t\t\te.Server = nil\n277: \t\t\treturn e, err\n278: \t\t}\n279: \t}\n280: \te.Server.Start()\n281: \n282: \te.servePeers()\n283: \n284: \te.serveClients()\n285: \n286: \tif err = e.serveMetrics(); err != nil {\n287: \t\treturn e, err\n288: \t}\n289: \n290: \te.cfg.logger.Info(\n291: \t\t\"now serving peer/client/metrics\",\n292: \t\tzap.String(\"local-member-id\", e.Server.MemberID().String()),\n293: \t\tzap.Strings(\"initial-advertise-peer-urls\", e.cfg.getAdvertisePeerURLs()),\n294: \t\tzap.Strings(\"listen-peer-urls\", e.cfg.getListenPeerURLs()),\n295: \t\tzap.Strings(\"advertise-client-urls\", e.cfg.getAdvertiseClientURLs()),\n296: \t\tzap.Strings(\"listen-client-urls\", e.cfg.getListenClientURLs()),\n297: \t\tzap.Strings(\"listen-metrics-urls\", e.cfg.getMetricsURLs()),\n298: \t)\n299: \tserving = true\n300: \treturn e, nil\n301: }\n302: \n303: func print(lg *zap.Logger, ec Config, sc config.ServerConfig, memberInitialized bool) {\n304: \tcors := make([]string, 0, Config returns the current configuration.\n389: func (e *Etcd) Config() Config {\n390: \treturn e.cfg\n391: }\n392: \n393: // Close gracefully shuts down all servers/listeners.\n394: // Client requests will be terminated with request timeout.\n395: // After timeout, enforce remaning requests be closed immediately.\n396: //\n397: // The rough workflow to shut down etcd:\n398: //  1. close the `stopc` channel, so that all error handlers (child\n399: //     goroutines) won't send back any errors anymore;\n400: //  2. stop the http and grpc servers gracefully, within request timeout;\n401: //  3. close all client and metrics listeners, so that etcd server\n402: //     stops receiving any new connection;\n403: //  4. call the cancel function to close the gateway context, so that\n404: //     all gateway connections are closed.\n405: //  5. stop etcd server gracefully, and ensure the main raft loop\n406: //     goroutine is stopped;\n407: //  6. stop all peer listeners, so that it stops receiving peer connections\n408: //     and messages (wait up to 1-second);\n409: //  7. wait for all child goroutines (i.e. client handlers, peer handlers\n410: //     and metrics handlers) to exit;\n411: //  8. close the `errc` channel to release the resource. Note that it's only\n412: //     safe to close the `errc` after step 7 above is done, otherwise the\n413: //     child goroutines may send errors back to already closed `errc` channel.\n414: func (e *Etcd) Close() {\n415: \tfields := []zap.Field{\n416: \t\tzap.String(\"name\", e.cfg.Name),\n417: \t\tzap.String(\"data-dir\", e.cfg.Dir),\n418: \t\tzap.Strings(\"advertise-peer-urls\", e.cfg.getAdvertisePeerURLs()),\n419: \t\tzap.Strings(\"advertise-client-urls\", e.cfg.getAdvertiseClientURLs()),\n420: \t}\n421: \tlg := e.GetLogger()\n422: \tlg.Info(\"closing etcd server\", fields...)\n423: \tdefer func() {\n424: \t\tlg.Info(\"closed etcd server\", fields...)\n425: \t\tverify.MustVerifyIfEnabled(verify.Config{\n426: \t\t\tLogger:     lg,\n427: \t\t\tDataDir:    e.cfg.Dir,\n428: \t\t\tExactIndex: false,\n429: \t\t})\n430: \t\tlg.Sync()\n431: \t}()\n432: \n433: \te.closeOnce.Do(func() {\n434: \t\tclose(e.stopc)\n435: \t})\n436: \n437: \t// close client requests with request timeout\n438: \ttimeout := 2 * time.Second\n439: \tif e.Server != nil {\n440: \t\ttimeout = e.Server.Cfg.ReqTimeout()\n441: \t}\n442: \tfor _, sctx := range e.sctxs {\n443: \t\tfor ss := range sctx.serversC {\n444: \t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)\n445: \t\t\tstopServers(ctx, ss)\n446: \t\t\tcancel()\n447: \t\t}\n448: \t}\n449: \n450: \tfor _, sctx := range e.sctxs {\n451: \t\tsctx.cancel()\n452: \t}\n453: \n454: \tfor i := range e.Clients {\n455: \t\tif e.Clients[i] != nil {\n456: \t\t\te.Clients[i].Close()\n457: \t\t}\n458: \t}\n459: \n460: \tfor i := range e.metricsListeners {\n461: \t\te.metricsListeners[i].Close()\n462: \t}\n463: \n464: \t// shutdown tracing exporter\n465: \tif e.tracingExporterShutdown != nil {\n466: \t\te.tracingExporterShutdown()\n467: \t}\n468: \n469: \t// close rafthttp transports\n470: \tif e.Server != nil {\n471: \t\te.Server.Stop()\n472: \t}\n473: \n474: \t// close all idle connections in peer handler (wait up to 1-second)\n475: \tfor i := range e.Peers {\n476: \t\tif e.Peers[i] != nil && e.Peers[i].close != nil {\n477: \t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), time.Second)\n478: \t\t\te.Peers[i].close(ctx)\n479: \t\t\tcancel()\n480: \t\t}\n481: \t}\n482: \tif e.errc != nil {\n483: \t\te.wg.Wait()\n484: \t\tclose(e.errc)\n485: \t}\n486: }\n487: \n488: func stopServers(ctx context.Context, ss *servers) {\n489: \t// first, close the http.Server\n490: \tif ss.http != nil {\n491: \t\tss.http.Shutdown(ctx)\n492: \t}\n493: \tif ss.grpc == nil {\n494: \t\treturn\n495: \t}\n496: \t// do not grpc.Server.GracefulStop when grpc runs under http server\n497: \t// See https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/issues/1384#issuecomment-317124531\n498: \t// and https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/issues/8916\n499: \tif ss.secure && ss.http != nil {\n500: \t\tss.grpc.Stop()\n501: \t\treturn\n502: \t}\n503: \n504: \tch := make(chan struct{})\n505: \tgo func() {\n506: \t\tdefer close(ch)\n507: \t\t// close listeners to stop accepting new connections,\n508: \t\t// will block on any existing transports\n509: \t\tss.grpc.GracefulStop()\n510: \t}()\n511: \n512: \t// wait until all pending RPCs are finished\n513: \tselect {\n514: \tcase <-ch:\n515: \tcase <-ctx.Done():\n516: \t\t// took too long, manually close open transports\n517: \t\t// e.g. watch streams\n518: \t\tss.grpc.Stop()\n519: \n520: \t\t// concurrent GracefulStop should be interrupted\n521: \t\t<-ch\n522: \t}\n523: }\n524: \n525: // Err - return channel used to report errors during etcd run/shutdown.\n526: // Since etcd 3.5 the channel is being closed when the etcd is over.\n527: func (e *Etcd) Err() <-chan error {\n528: \treturn e.errc\n529: }\n530: \n531: func configurePeerListeners(cfg *Config) (peers []*peerListener, err error) {\n532: \tif err = updateCipherSuites(&cfg.PeerTLSInfo, cfg.CipherSuites); err != nil {\n533: \t\treturn nil, err\n534: \t}\n535: \tif err = cfg.PeerSelfCert(); err != nil {\n536: \t\tcfg.logger.Fatal(\"failed to get peer self-signed certs\", zap.Error(err))\n537: \t}\n538: \tupdateMinMaxVersions(&cfg.PeerTLSInfo, cfg.TlsMinVersion, cfg.TlsMaxVersion)\n539: \tif !cfg.PeerTLSInfo.Empty() {\n540: \t\tcfg.logger.Info(\n541: \t\t\t\"starting with peer TLS\",\n542: \t\t\tzap.String(\"tls-info\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%+v\", cfg.PeerTLSInfo)),\n543: \t\t\tzap.Strings(\"cipher-suites\", cfg.CipherSuites),\n544: \t\t)\n545: \t}\n546: \n547: \tpeers =
