\t\t\t\t\tzap.Error(err),\n558: \t\t\t\t)\n559: \t\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), time.Second)\n560: \t\t\t\tpeers[i].close(ctx)\n561: \t\t\t\tcancel()\n562: \t\t\t}\n563: \t\t}\n564: \t}()\n565: \n566: \tfor i, u := range cfg.ListenPeerUrls {\n567: \t\tif u.Scheme == \"http\" {\n568: \t\t\tif !cfg.PeerTLSInfo.Empty() {\n569: \t\t\t\tcfg.logger.Warn(\"scheme is HTTP while key and cert files are present; ignoring key and cert files\", zap.String(\"peer-url\", u.String()))\n570: \t\t\t}\n571: \t\t\tif cfg.PeerTLSInfo.ClientCertAuth {\n572: \t\t\t\tcfg.logger.Warn(\"scheme is HTTP while --peer-client-cert-auth is enabled; ignoring client cert auth for this URL\", zap.String(\"peer-url\", u.String()))\n573: \t\t\t}\n574: \t\t}\n575: \t\tpeers[i] = &peerListener{close: func(context.Context) error { return nil }}\n576: \t\tpeers[i].Listener, err = transport.NewListenerWithOpts(u.Host, u.Scheme,\n577: \t\t\ttransport.WithTLSInfo(&cfg.PeerTLSInfo),\n578: \t\t\ttransport.WithSocketOpts(&cfg.SocketOpts),\n579: \t\t\ttransport.WithTimeout(rafthttp.ConnReadTimeout, rafthttp.ConnWriteTimeout),\n580: \t\t)\n581: \t\tif err != nil {\n582: \t\t\tcfg.logger.Error(\"creating peer listener failed\", zap.Error(err))\n583: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n584: \t\t}\n585: \t\t// once serve, overwrite with 'http.Server.Shutdown'\n586: \t\tpeers[i].close = func(context.Context) error {\n587: \t\t\treturn peers[i].Listener.Close()\n588: \t\t}\n589: \t}\n590: \treturn peers, nil\n591: }\n592: \n593: // configure peer handlers after rafthttp.Transport started\n594: func (e *Etcd) servePeers() {\n595: \tph := etcdhttp.NewPeerHandler(e.GetLogger(), e.Server)\n596: \n597: \tfor _, p := range e.Peers {\n598: \t\tu := p.Listener.Addr().String()\n599: \t\tm := cmux.New(p.Listener)\n600: \t\tsrv := &http.Server{\n601: \t\t\tHandler:     ph,\n602: \t\t\tReadTimeout: 5 * time.Minute,\n603: \t\t\tErrorLog:    defaultLog.New(io.Discard, \"\", 0), // do not log user error\n604: \t\t}\n605: \t\tgo srv.Serve(m.Match(cmux.Any()))\n606: \t\tp.serve = func() error {\n607: \t\t\te.cfg.logger.Info(\n608: \t\t\t\t\"cmux::serve\",\n609: \t\t\t\tzap.String(\"address\", u),\n610: \t\t\t)\n611: \t\t\treturn m.Serve()\n612: \t\t}\n613: \t\tp.close = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n614: \t\t\t// gracefully shutdown http.Server\n615: \t\t\t// close open listeners, idle connections\n616: \t\t\t// until context cancel or time-out\n617: \t\t\te.cfg.logger.Info(\n618: \t\t\t\t\"stopping serving peer traffic\",\n619: \t\t\t\tzap.String(\"address\", u),\n620: \t\t\t)\n621: \t\t\tsrv.Shutdown(ctx)\n622: \t\t\te.cfg.logger.Info(\n623: \t\t\t\t\"stopped serving peer traffic\",\n624: \t\t\t\tzap.String(\"address\", u),\n625: \t\t\t)\n626: \t\t\tm.Close()\n627: \t\t\treturn nil\n628: \t\t}\n629: \t}\n630: \n631: \t// start peer servers in a goroutine\n632: \tfor _, \t\t\t\tzap.String(\"address\", u),\n639: \t\t\t)\n640: \t\t\treturn l.serve()\n641: \t\t})\n642: \t}\n643: }\n644: \n645: func configureClientListeners(cfg *Config) (sctxs map[string]*serveCtx, err error) {\n646: \tif err = updateCipherSuites(&cfg.ClientTLSInfo, cfg.CipherSuites); err != nil {\n647: \t\treturn nil, err\n648: \t}\n649: \tif err = cfg.ClientSelfCert(); err != nil {\n650: \t\tcfg.logger.Fatal(\"failed to get client self-signed certs\", zap.Error(err))\n651: \t}\n652: \tupdateMinMaxVersions(&cfg.ClientTLSInfo, cfg.TlsMinVersion, cfg.TlsMaxVersion)\n653: \tif append(cfg.ListenClientUrls, cfg.ListenClientHttpUrls...) {\n659: \t\tif u.Scheme == \"http\" || u.Scheme == \"unix\" {\n660: \t\t\tif !cfg.ClientTLSInfo.Empty() {\n661: \t\t\t\tcfg.logger.Warn(\"scheme is http or unix while key and cert files are present; ignoring key and cert files\", zap.String(\"client-url\", u.String()))\n662: \t\t\t}\n663: \t\t\tif u.String()))\n665: \t\t\t}\n666: \t\t}\n667: \t\tif (u.Scheme == \"https\" || u.Scheme == \"unixs\") && cfg.ClientTLSInfo.Empty() {\n668: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"TLS key/cert (--cert-file, --key-file) must be provided for client url %s with HTTPS scheme\", u.String())\n669: \t\t}\n670: \t}\n671: \n672: \tfor _, u := range cfg.ListenClientUrls {\n673: \t\taddr, secure, network := resolveURL(u)\n674: \t\tsctx := sctxs[addr]\n675: \t\tif sctx == nil {\n676: \t\t\tsctx = newServeCtx(cfg.logger)\n677: \t\t\tsctxs[addr] = sctx\n678: \t\t}\n679: \t\tsctx.secure = sctx.secure || cfg.ListenClientHttpUrls {\n686: \t\taddr, secure, network := resolveURL(u)\n687: \n688: \t\tsctx := sctxs[addr]\n689: \t\tif sctx == nil {\n690: \t\t\tsctx = newServeCtx(cfg.logger)\n691: \t\t\tsctxs[addr] = sctx\n692: \t\t} else if !sctx.httpOnly {\n693: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot bind both --listen-client-urls and --listen-client-http-urls on the same url %s\", u.String())\n694: \t\t}\n695: \t\tsctx.secure = sctx.secure || secure\n696: \t\tsctx.insecure = sctx.insecure || !secure\n697: \t\tsctx.scheme = u.Scheme\n698: \t\tsctx.addr = addr\n699: \t\tsctx.network = network\n700: \t\tsctx.httpOnly = true\n701: \t}\n702: \n703: \tfor _, sctx := range sctxs {\n704: \t\tif sctx.l, err = transport.NewListenerWithOpts(sctx.addr, sctx.scheme,\n705: \t\t\ttransport.WithSocketOpts(&cfg.SocketOpts),\n706: \t\t\ttransport.WithSkipTLSInfoCheck(true),\n707: \t\t); err != nil {\n708: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n709: \t\t}\n710: \t\t// net.Listener will rewrite ipv4 0.0.0.0 to ipv6 [::], breaking\n711: \t\t// hosts that disable ipv6. So, use the address given by the user.\n712: \n713: \t\tif fdLimit, fderr := runtimeutil.FDLimit(); fderr == nil {\n714: \t\t\tif fdLimit <= reservedInternalFDNum {\n715: \t\t\t\tcfg.logger.Fatal(\n716: \t\t\t\t\t\"file descriptor limit of etcd process is too low; please set higher\",\n717: \t\t\t\t\tzap.Uint64(\"limit\", fdLimit),\n718: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc\"\n26: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n27: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\"\n28: \tgrpchealth \"google.golang.org/grpc/health/grpc_health_v1\"\n29: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/metadata\"\n30: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/component-base/version\"\n32: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n33: \t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/probe\"\n34: )\n35: \n36: // Prober is an interface that defines the Probe function for doing GRPC readiness/liveness/startup checks.\n37: type Prober interface {\n38: \tProbe(host, service string, port int, timeout time.Duration) (probe.Result, string, error)\n39: }\n40: \n41: type grpcProber struct {\n42: }\n43: \n44: // New Prober for execute grpc probe\n45: func New() Prober {\n46: \treturn grpcProber{}\n47: }\n48: \n49: // Probe executes a grpc call to check the liveness/readiness/startup of container.\n50: // Returns the Result status, command output, and errors if any.\n51: // Any failure is considered as a probe failure to mimic grpc_health_probe tool behavior.\n52: // err is always nil\n53: func (p grpcProber) Probe(host, service string, port int, timeout time.Duration) (probe.Result, string, error) {\n54: \tv := version.Get()\n55: \n56: \topts := []grpc.DialOption{\n57: \t\tgrpc.WithUserAgent(fmt.Sprintf(\"kube-probe/%s.%s\", v.Major, v.Minor)),\n58: \t\tgrpc.WithBlock(),\n59: \t\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()), //credentials are currently not supported\n60: \t\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(ctx context.Context, addr string) (net.Conn, error) {\n61: \t\t\treturn probe.ProbeDialer().DialContext(ctx, \"tcp\", addr)\n62: \t\t}),\n63: \t}\n64: \n65: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)\n66: \n67: \tdefer cancel()\n68: \n69: \taddr := net.JoinHostPort(host, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", port))\n70: \tconn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, opts...)\n71: \n72: \tif err != nil {\n73: \t\tif err == context.DeadlineExceeded {\n74: \t\t\tklog.V(4).ErrorS(err, \"failed to connect grpc service due to timeout\", \"addr\", addr, \"service\", service, \"timeout\", timeout)\n75: \t\t\treturn probe.Failure, fmt.Sprintf(\"timeout: failed to connect service %q within %v: %+v\", addr, timeout, err), nil\n76: \t\t} else {\n77: \t\t\tklog.V(4).ErrorS(err, \"failed to connect grpc service\", \"service\", addr)\n78: \t\t\treturn probe.Failure, fmt.Sprintf(\"error: failed to connect service at %q: %+v\", addr, err), nil\n79: \t\t}\n80: \t}\n81: \n82: \tdefer func() {\n83: \t\t_ = conn.Close()\n84: \t}()\n85: \n86: \tclient := grpchealth.NewHealthClient(conn)\n87: \n88: \tresp, err := client.Check(metadata.NewOutgoingContext(ctx, make(metadata.MD)), &grpchealth.HealthCheckRequest{\n89: \t\tService: service,\n90: \t})\n91: \n92: \tif err != nil {\n93: \t\tstat, ok := status.FromError(err)\n94: \t\tif ok {\n95: \t\t\tswitch stat.Code() {\n96: \t\t\tcase codes.Unimplemented:\n97: \t\t\t\tklog.V(4).ErrorS(err, \"server does not implement the grpc health protocol (grpc.health.v1.Health)\", \"addr\", addr, \"service\", service)\n98: \t\t\t\treturn probe.Failure, fmt.Sprintf(\"error: this server does not implement the grpc health protocol (grpc.health.v1.Health): %s\", stat.Message()), nil\n99: \t\t\tcase codes.DeadlineExceeded:\n100: \t\t\t\tklog.V(4).ErrorS(err, \"rpc request not finished within timeout\", \"addr\", addr, \"service\", service, \"timeout\", timeout)\n101: \t\t\t\treturn probe.Failure, fmt.Sprintf(\"timeout: health rpc did not complete within %v\", timeout), nil\n102: \t\t\tdefault:\n103: \t\t\t\tklog.V(4).ErrorS(err, \"rpc probe failed\")\n104: \t\t\t}\n105: \t\t} else {\n106: \t\t\tklog.V(4).ErrorS(err, \"health rpc probe failed\")\n107: \t\t}\n108: \n109: \t\treturn probe.Failure, fmt.Sprintf(\"error: health rpc probe failed: %+v\", err), nil\n110: \t}\n111: \n112: \tif resp.GetStatus() != grpchealth.HealthCheckResponse_SERVING {\n113: \t\treturn probe.Failure, fmt.Sprintf(\"service unhealthy (responded with %q)\", resp.GetStatus().String()), nil\n114: \t}\n115: \n116: \treturn probe.Success, fmt.Sprintf(\"service healthy\"), nil\n117: }\n118: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--8c9c67c0@8c9c67c000104450cfc5a5f48053a9a84b73cf93/-/blob/pkg/probe/grpc/grpc.go"}}}

https://github.com/container-storage-interface/spec/blob/master/spec.md\\n718: \\tst, ok := status.FromError(err)\\n719: \\tif !ok {\\n720: \\t\\t// This is not gRPC error. When CSI clients have not been discovered yet (e.g.\\n550: // on kubelet restart), client initialization will fail. Users of CSI client (e.g.\\n551: // mounter manager and block mapper) can use this to delay CSI client\\n552: // initialization until needed.\\n553: type csiClientGetter struct {\\n554: \\tsync.RWMutex\\n555: \\tcsiClient  csiClient\\n556: \\tdriverName csiDriverName\\n557: }\\n558: \\n559: func (c *csiClientGetter) Get() (csiClient, error) {\\n560: \\tc.RLock()\\n561: \\tif resource.BinarySI)\\n652: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.Capacity = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetTotal(), resource.BinarySI)\\n653: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.Used = resource.NewQuantity(usage.GetUsed(), \\n670: func (c *csiDriverClient) NodeSupportsVolumeMountGroup(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\\n671: \\treturn c.nodeSupportsCapability(ctx, csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability_RPC_Type) (bool, error) {\\n675: \\tklog.V(4).Info(log(\\\"calling NodeGetCapabilities rpc to determine if the node service has %s capability\\\", capabilityType))\\n676: \\tcapabilities, err := c.nodeGetCapabilities(ctx)\\n677: \\tif err != nil {\\n678: \\t\\treturn false, err\\n679: \\t}\\n680: \\n681: \\tfor _, capability := range capabilities {\\n682: \\t\\tif \\t\\t}\\n688: \\t}\\n689: \\treturn false, nil\\n690: }\\n691: \\n692: func (c *csiDriverClient) nodeGetCapabilities(ctx context.Context) ([]*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability, error) {\\n693: \\tif c.nodeV1ClientCreator == nil {\\n694: \\t\\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, errors.New(\\\"nodeV1ClientCreate is nil\\\")\\n695: \\t}\\n696: \\n697: \\tnodeClient, closer, err := c.nodeV1ClientCreator(c.addr, c.metricsManager)\\n698: \\tif err != nil {\\n699: \\t\\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, err\\n700: \\t}\\n701: \\tdefer closer.Close()\\n702: \\n703: \\treq := &csipbv1.NodeGetCapabilitiesRequest{}\\n704: \\tresp, err := nodeClient.NodeGetCapabilities(ctx, req)\\n705: \\tif err != nil {\\n706: \\t\\tif !isFinalError(err) {\\n707: \\t\\t\\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, volumetypes.NewUncertainProgressError(err.Error())\\n708: \\t\\t}\\n709: \\t\\treturn []*csipbv1.NodeServiceCapability{}, err\\n710: \\t}\\n711: \\treturn resp.GetCapabilities(), nil\\n712: }\\n713: \\n714: func isFinalError(err error) bool {\\n715: \\t// Sources:\\n716: \\t// https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/statuscodes.md\\n717: \\t// https://github.com/container-storage-interface/spec/blob/master/spec.md\\n718: \\tst, ok := status.FromError(err)\\n719: \\tif !ok {\\n720: \\t\\t// This is not gRPC error. The operation must have failed before gRPC\\n721: \\t\\t// method was called, otherwise we would get gRPC error.\\n722: \\t\\t// We don't know if any previous volume operation is in progress, be on the safe side.\\n723: \\t\\treturn false\\n724: \\t}\\n725: \\tswitch st.Code() {\\n726: \\tcase codes.Canceled, // gRPC: Client Application cancelled the request\\n727: \\t\\tcodes.DeadlineExceeded,  // gRPC: Timeout\\n728: \\t\\tcodes.Unavailable,       // gRPC: Server shutting down, TCP connection broken - previous volume operation may be still in progress.\\n729: \\t\\tcodes.ResourceExhausted, // gRPC: Server temporarily out of resources - previous volume operation may be still in progress.\\n730: \\t\\tcodes.Aborted:           // CSI: Operation pending for volume\\n731: \\t\\treturn false\\n732: \\t}\\n733: \\t// All other errors mean that operation either did not\\n734: \\t// even start or failed. It is for sure not in progress.\\n735: \\treturn true\\n736: }\\n737: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--8c9c67c0@8c9c67c000104450cfc5a5f48053a9a84b73cf93/-/blob/pkg/volume/csi/csi_client.go\"}","structuredContent":{"content":"1: (nodeV1AccessModeMapper, error) {\n491: \tsupported, err := c.NodeSupportsSingleNodeMultiWriterAccessMode(ctx)\n492: \tif err != nil {\n493: \t\treturn nil, err\n494: \t}\n495: \tif supported {\n496: \t\treturn asSingleNodeMultiWriterCapableCSIAccessModeV1, nil\n497: \t}\n498: \treturn asCSIAccessModeV1, nil\n499: }\n500: \n501: func asCSIAccessModeV1(am api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode) csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_Mode {\n502: \tswitch am {\n503: \tcase api.ReadWriteOnce:\n504: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_WRITER\n505: \tcase api.ReadOnlyMany:\n506: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_READER_ONLY\n507: \tcase api.ReadWriteMany:\n508: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\n509: \t// This mapping exists to enable CSI drivers that lack the\n510: \t// SINGLE_NODE_MULTI_WRITER capability to work with the\n511: \t// ReadWriteOncePod access mode.\n512: \tcase api.ReadWriteOncePod:\n513: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_WRITER\n514: \t}\n515: \treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_UNKNOWN\n516: }\n517: \n518: func asSingleNodeMultiWriterCapableCSIAccessModeV1(am api.PersistentVolumeAccessMode) csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_Mode {\n519: \tswitch am {\n520: \tcase api.ReadWriteOnce:\n521: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\n522: \tcase api.ReadOnlyMany:\n523: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_READER_ONLY\n524: \tcase api.ReadWriteMany:\n525: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_MULTI_NODE_MULTI_WRITER\n526: \tcase api.ReadWriteOncePod:\n527: \t\treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_SINGLE_NODE_SINGLE_WRITER\n528: \t}\n529: \treturn csipbv1.VolumeCapability_AccessMode_UNKNOWN\n530: }\n531: \n532: func newGrpcConn(addr csiAddr, metricsManager *MetricsManager) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n533: \tnetwork := \"unix\"\n534: \tklog.V(4).InfoS(log(\"creating new gRPC connection\"), \"protocol\", network, \"endpoint\", addr)\n535: \n536: \treturn grpc.Dial(\n537: \t\tstring(addr),\n538: \t\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\"localhost\"),\n539: \t\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\n540: \t\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(ctx context.Context, target string) (net.Conn, error) {\n541: \t\t\treturn (&net.Dialer{}).DialContext(ctx, network, target)\n542: \t\t}),\n543: \t\tgrpc.WithChainUnaryInterceptor(metricsManager.RecordMetricsInterceptor),\n544: \t)\n545: }\n546: \n547: // CSI client getter with cache.\n548: // This provides a method to initialize CSI client with driver name and caches\n549: // it for later use. When CSI clients have not been discovered yet (e.g.\n550: // on kubelet restart), client initialization will fail. isFinalError(err error) bool {\n715: \t// Sources:\n716: \t// https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/statuscodes.md\n717: \t// https://github.com/container-storage-interface/spec/blob/master/spec.md\n718: \tst, ok := status.FromError(err)\n719: \tif !ok {\n720: \t\t// This is not gRPC error. The operation must have failed before gRPC\n721: \t\t// method was called, otherwise we would get gRPC error.\n722: \t\t// We don't know if any previous volume operation is in progress, be on the safe side.\n723: \t\treturn false\n724: \t}\n725: \tswitch st.Code() {\n726: \tcase codes.Canceled, // gRPC: Client Application cancelled the request\n727: \t\tcodes.DeadlineExceeded,  // gRPC: Timeout\n728: \t\tcodes.Unavailable,       // gRPC: Server shutting down, TCP connection broken - previous volume operation may be still in progress.\n729: \t\tcodes.ResourceExhausted, // gRPC: Server temporarily out of resources - previous volume operation may be still in progress.\n730: \t\tcodes.Aborted:           // CSI: Operation pending for volume\n731: \t\treturn false\n732: \t}\n733: \t// All other errors mean that operation either did not\n734: \t// even start or failed. string\\n69: \\n70: var (\\n71: \\t// ErrContainerStatusNil indicates that the returned container status is nil.\\n72: \\tErrContainerStatusNil = errors.New(\\\"container status is nil\\\")\\n73: \\n74: \\t// ErrCommandTimedOut indicates that the exec sync command timed.\\n75: \\tErrCommandTimedOut = errors.New(\\\"command timed out\\\")\\n76: )\\n77: \\n78: const (\\n79: \\t// CRIVersionV1 references the v1 CRI API.\\n80: \\tCRIVersionV1 CRIVersion = \\\"v1\\\"\\n81: )\\n82: \\n83: // NewRemoteRuntimeService creates a new internalapi.RuntimeService.\\n84: func NewRemoteRuntimeService(endpoint string, connectionTimeout time.Duration, tp trace.TracerProvider, logger *klog.Logger) (internalapi.RuntimeService, error) {\\n85: \\tinternal.Log(logger, 3, \\\"Connecting to runtime service\\\", \\\"endpoint\\\", endpoint)\\n86: \\taddr, dialer, err := util.GetAddressAndDialer(endpoint)\\n87: \\tif err != nil {\\n88: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n89: \\t}\\n90: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), connectionTimeout)\\n91: \\tdefer cancel()\\n92: \\n93: \\tvar dialOpts []grpc.DialOption\\n94: \\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n95: \\t\\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\\n96: \\t\\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\\\"localhost\\\"),\\n97: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(dialer),\\n98: \\t\\tgrpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(maxMsgSize)))\\n99: \\tif tp != nil {\\n100: \\t\\ttracingOpts := []otelgrpc.Option{\\n101: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithMessageEvents(otelgrpc.ReceivedEvents, otelgrpc.SentEvents),\\n102: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithPropagators(tracing.Propagators()),\\n103: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithTracerProvider(tp),\\n104: \\t\\t}\\n105: \\t\\t// Even if there is no TracerProvider, the otelgrpc still handles context propagation.\\n106: \\t\\t// See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-go/tree/main/example/passthrough\\n107: \\t\\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n108: \\t\\t\\tgrpc.WithStatsHandler(otelgrpc.NewClientHandler(tracingOpts...)))\\n109: \\t}\\n110: \\n111: \\tconnParams := grpc.ConnectParams{\\n112: \\t\\tBackoff: backoff.DefaultConfig,\\n113: \\t}\\n114: \\tconnParams.MinConnectTimeout = minConnectionTimeout\\n115: \\tconnParams.Backoff.BaseDelay = baseBackoffDelay\\n116: \\tconnParams.Backoff.MaxDelay = maxBackoffDelay\\n117: \\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n118: \\t\\tgrpc.WithConnectParams(connParams),\\n119: \\t)\\n120: \\n121: \\tconn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, dialOpts...)\\n122: \\tif err != nil {\\n123: \\t\\tinternal.LogErr(logger, err, \\\"Connect remote runtime failed\\\", \\\"address\\\", addr)\\n124: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n125: \\t}\\n126: \\n127: \\tservice := &remoteRuntimeService{\\n128: \\t\\ttimeout:      connectionTimeout,\\n129: \\t\\tlogReduction: logreduction.NewLogReduction(identicalErrorDelay),\\n130: \\t\\tlogger:       logger,\\n131: \\t\\tconn:         conn,\\n132: \\t}\\n133: \\n134: \\tif err := service.validateServiceConnection(ctx, conn, endpoint); err != nil {\\n135: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"validate service connection: %w\\\", err)\\n136: \\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\treturn service, nil\\n139: }\\n140: \\n141: // Close will shutdown the internal gRPC client connection.\\n142: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Close() error {\\n143: \\tr.log(3, \\\"Closing runtime service connection\\\")\\n144: \\treturn r.conn.Close()\\n145: }\\n146: \\n147: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) log(level int, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\\n148: err, msg, keyAndValues...)\\n153: }\\n154: \\n155: // validateServiceConnection tries to connect to the remote runtime service by\\n156: // using the CRI v1 API version and fails if that's not output.\\n505: // If command exits with a non-zero exit code, an error is returned.\\n506: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ExecSync(ctx context.Context, containerID string, cmd []string, timeout time.Duration) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) {\\n507: \\tr.log(10, \\\"[RemoteRuntimeService] ExecSync\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"timeout\\\", timeout)\\n508: \\t// Do not \\tdefer cancel()\\n518: \\n519: \\treturn r.execSyncV1(ctx, containerID, cmd, timeout)\\n520: }\\n521: \\n522: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) execSyncV1(ctx context.Context, containerID string, cmd []string, timeout time.Duration) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) {\\n523: \\ttimeoutSeconds := int64(timeout.Seconds())\\n524: \\treq := &runtimeapi.ExecSyncRequest{\\n525: \\t\\tContainerId: containerID,\\n526: \\t\\tCmd:         cmd,\\n527: \\t\\tTimeout:     timeoutSeconds,\\n528: \\t}\\n529: \\tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ExecSync(ctx, req)\\n530: \\tif err != nil internalapi.RuntimeService.\\n84: func NewRemoteRuntimeService(endpoint string, connectionTimeout time.Duration, tp trace.TracerProvider, logger *klog.Logger) (internalapi.RuntimeService, error) {\\n85: \\tinternal.Log(logger, 3, \\\"Connecting to runtime service\\\", \\\"endpoint\\\", endpoint)\\n86: \\taddr, dialer, err := util.GetAddressAndDialer(endpoint)\\n87: \\tif err != nil {\\n88: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n89: \\t}\\n90: \\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), connectionTimeout)\\n91: \\tdefer cancel()\\n92: \\n93: \\tvar dialOpts []grpc.DialOption\\n94: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(dialer),\\n98: \\t\\tgrpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(maxMsgSize)))\\n99: \\tif tp != nil {\\n100: \\t\\ttracingOpts := []otelgrpc.Option{\\n101: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithMessageEvents(otelgrpc.ReceivedEvents, otelgrpc.SentEvents),\\n102: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithPropagators(tracing.Propagators()),\\n103: \\t\\t\\totelgrpc.WithTracerProvider(tp),\\n104: \\t\\t}\\n105: \\t\\t// Even if there is no TracerProvider, the otelgrpc still handles context propagation.\\n106: \\t\\t// See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-go/tree/main/example/passthrough\\n107: \\t\\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n108: \\t\\t\\tgrpc.WithStatsHandler(otelgrpc.NewClientHandler(tracingOpts...)))\\n109: \\t}\\n110: \\n111: \\tconnParams := grpc.ConnectParams{\\n112: \\t\\tBackoff: backoff.DefaultConfig,\\n113: \\t}\\n114: \\tconnParams.MinConnectTimeout = minConnectionTimeout\\n115: \\tconnParams.Backoff.BaseDelay = baseBackoffDelay\\n116: \\tconnParams.Backoff.MaxDelay = maxBackoffDelay\\n117: \\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\\n118: \\t\\tgrpc.WithConnectParams(connParams),\\n119: \\t)\\n120: \\n121: \\tconn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, dialOpts...)\\n122: \\tif err != nil {\\n123: \\t\\tinternal.LogErr(logger, err, \\\"Connect remote runtime failed\\\", \\\"address\\\", addr)\\n124: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n125: \\t}\\n126: \\n127: \\tservice := &remoteRuntimeService{\\n128: \\t\\ttimeout:      connectionTimeout,\\n129: \\t\\tlogReduction: logreduction.NewLogReduction(identicalErrorDelay),\\n130: \\t\\tlogger:       logger,\\n131: \\t\\tconn:         conn,\\n132: \\t}\\n133: \\n134: \\tif err := service.validateServiceConnection(ctx, conn, endpoint); err != nil {\\n135: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"validate service connection: %w\\\", err)\\n136: \\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\treturn service, nil\\n139: }\\n140: \\n141: // Close will shutdown the internal gRPC client connection.\\n142: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Close() error {\\n143: \\tr.log(3, \\\"Closing runtime service connection\\\")\\n144: \\treturn r.conn.Close()\\n145: }\\n146: \\n147: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) log(level int, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\\n148: \\tinternal.Log(r.logger, level, msg, keyAndValues...)\\n149: }\\n150: \\n151: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) logErr(err error, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\\n152: \\tinternal.LogErr(r.logger, err, msg, keyAndValues...)\\n153: }\\n154: \\n155: // validateServiceConnection tries to connect to the remote runtime service by\\n156: // using the CRI v1 API version and fails if that's not possible.\\n157: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) validateServiceConnection(ctx context.Context, conn *grpc.ClientConn, endpoint string) error {\\n158: \\tr.log(4, \\\"Validating the CRI v1 API runtime }\\n168: \\n169: // Version returns the runtime name, runtime version and runtime API version.\\n170: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Version(ctx context.Context, apiVersion string) \n46: // remoteRuntimeService is a gRPC implementation of internalapi.RuntimeService.\n47: type remoteRuntimeService struct {\n48: \ttimeout       time.Duration\n49: \truntimeClient runtimeapi.RuntimeServiceClient\n50: \t// Cache last per-container error message to reduce log spam\n51: \tlogReduction *logreduction.LogReduction\n52: \tlogger       *klog.Logger\n53: \tconn         *grpc.ClientConn\n54: }\n55: \n56: const (\n57: \t// How frequently to report identical errors\n58: \tidenticalErrorDelay = 1 * time.Minute\n59: \n60: \t// connection parameters\n61: \tmaxBackoffDelay      = 3 * time.Second\n62: \tbaseBackoffDelay     = 100 * time.Millisecond\n63: \tminConnectionTimeout = 5 * time.Second\n64: )\n65: \n66: // CRIVersion is the type for valid Container Runtime Interface (CRI) API\n67: // versions.\n68: type CRIVersion string\n69: \n70: var (\n71: \t// ErrContainerStatusNil indicates that the returned container status is nil.\n72: \tErrContainerStatusNil = errors.New(\"container status is nil\")\n73: \n74: \t// ErrCommandTimedOut indicates that the exec sync command timed.\n75: \tErrCommandTimedOut = errors.New(\"command timed out\")\n76: )\n77: \n78: const (\n79: \t// CRIVersionV1 references the v1 CRI API.\n80: \tCRIVersionV1 CRIVersion = \"v1\"\n81: )\n82: \n83: // NewRemoteRuntimeService creates a new internalapi.RuntimeService.\n84: func NewRemoteRuntimeService(endpoint string, connectionTimeout time.Duration, tp trace.TracerProvider, logger *klog.Logger) (internalapi.RuntimeService, error) {\n85: \tinternal.Log(logger, 3, \"Connecting to runtime service\", \"endpoint\", endpoint)\n86: \taddr, dialer, err := util.GetAddressAndDialer(endpoint)\n87: \tif err != nil {\n88: \t\treturn nil, err\n89: \t}\n90: \tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), connectionTimeout)\n91: \tdefer cancel()\n92: \n93: \tvar dialOpts []grpc.DialOption\n94: \tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n95: \t\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\n96: \t\tgrpc.WithAuthority(\"localhost\"),\n97: \t\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(dialer),\n98: \t\tgrpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(maxMsgSize)))\n99: \tif tp != nil {\n100: \t\ttracingOpts := []otelgrpc.Option{\n101: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithMessageEvents(otelgrpc.ReceivedEvents, otelgrpc.SentEvents),\n102: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithPropagators(tracing.Propagators()),\n103: \t\t\totelgrpc.WithTracerProvider(tp),\n104: \t\t}\n105: \t\t// Even if there is no TracerProvider, the otelgrpc still handles context propagation.\n106: \t\t// See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-go/tree/main/example/passthrough\n107: \t\tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n108: \t\t\tgrpc.WithStatsHandler(otelgrpc.NewClientHandler(tracingOpts...)))\n109: \t}\n110: \n111: \tconnParams := grpc.ConnectParams{\n112: \t\tBackoff: backoff.DefaultConfig,\n113: \t}\n114: \tconnParams.MinConnectTimeout = minConnectionTimeout\n115: \tconnParams.Backoff.BaseDelay = baseBackoffDelay\n116: \tconnParams.Backoff.MaxDelay = maxBackoffDelay\n117: \tdialOpts = append(dialOpts,\n118: \t\tgrpc.WithConnectParams(connParams),\n119: \t)\n120: \n121: \tconn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, dialOpts...)\n122: \tif err != nil {\n123: \t\tinternal.LogErr(logger, err, \"Connect remote runtime failed\", \"address\", addr)\n124: \t\treturn nil, err\n125: \t}\n126: \n127: \tservice := &remoteRuntimeService{\n128: \t\ttimeout:      connectionTimeout,\n129: \t\tlogReduction: logreduction.NewLogReduction(identicalErrorDelay),\n130: \t\tlogger:       logger,\n131: \t\tconn:         conn,\n132: \t}\n133: \n134: \tif err := service.validateServiceConnection(ctx, conn, endpoint); err != nil {\n135: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"validate service connection: %w\", err)\n136: \t}\n137: \n138: \treturn service, nil\n139: }\n140: \n141: // Close will shutdown the internal gRPC client connection.\n142: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) Close() error {\n143: \tr.log(3, \"Closing runtime service connection\")\n144: \treturn r.conn.Close()\n145: }\n146: \n147: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) log(level int, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\n148: \tinternal.Log(r.logger, level, msg, keyAndValues...)\n149: }\n150: \n151: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) logErr(err error, msg string, keyAndValues ...any) {\n152: \tinternal.LogErr(r.logger, err, msg, keyAndValues...)\n153: }\n154: \n155: // validateServiceConnection tries to connect to the remote runtime service by\n156: // using the CRI v1 API version and fails if that's not possible.\n157: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) validateServiceConnection(ctx context.Context, conn *grpc.ClientConn, endpoint string) error {\n158: \tr.log(4, \"Validating the CRI v1 API runtime version\")\n159: \tr.runtimeClient = runtimeapi.NewRuntimeServiceClient(conn)\n160: \n161: \tif _, err := r.runtimeClient.Version(ctx, &runtimeapi.VersionRequest{}); err != nil {\n162: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"validate CRI v1 runtime API for endpoint %q: %w\", endpoint, err)\n163: \t}\n164: \n165: \tr.log(2, \"Validated CRI v1 runtime API\")\n166: \treturn nil\n167: }\n168: \n169: // Version returns the runtime name, runtime version and runtime API version.\n170: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) // ExecSync executes a command in the container, and returns the stdout output.\n505: // If command exits with a non-zero exit code, an error is returned.\n506: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) ExecSync(ctx context.Context, containerID string, cmd []string, timeout time.Duration) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) {\n507: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ExecSync\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"timeout\", timeout)\n508: \t// Do not set timeout when timeout is 0.\n509: \tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n510: \tif timeout != 0 {\n511: \t\t// Use timeout + default timeout (2 minutes) as timeout to leave some time for\n512: \t\t// the runtime to do cleanup.\n513: \t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.timeout+timeout)\n514: \t} else {\n515: \t\tctx, cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)\n516: \t}\n517: \tdefer cancel()\n518: \n519: \treturn r.execSyncV1(ctx, containerID, cmd, timeout)\n520: }\n521: \n522: func (r *remoteRuntimeService) execSyncV1(ctx context.Context, containerID string, cmd []string, timeout time.Duration) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) {\n523: \ttimeoutSeconds := int64(timeout.Seconds())\n524: \treq := &runtimeapi.ExecSyncRequest{\n525: \t\tContainerId: containerID,\n526: \t\tCmd:         cmd,\n527: \t\tTimeout:     timeoutSeconds,\n528: \t}\n529: \tresp, err := r.runtimeClient.ExecSync(ctx, req)\n530: \tif err != nil {\n531: \t\tr.logErr(err, \"ExecSync cmd from runtime service failed\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"cmd\", cmd)\n532: \n533: \t\t// interpret DeadlineExceeded gRPC errors as timedout errors\n534: \t\tif status.Code(err) == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\n535: \t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"%w: %q timed out after %s\", ErrCommandTimedOut, strings.Join(cmd, \" \"), timeout)\n536: \t\t}\n537: \n538: \t\treturn nil, nil, err\n539: \t}\n540: \n541: \tr.log(10, \"[RemoteRuntimeService] ExecSync Response\", \"containerID\", containerID, \"exitCode\", resp.ExitCode)\n542: \terr = nil\n543: \tif resp.ExitCode != 0 {\n544: \t\terr = utilexec.CodeExitError{\n545: \t\t\tErr:  fmt.Errorf(\"command '%s' exited with %d: %s\", strings.Join(cmd, \" \"), resp.ExitCode, resp.Stderr),\n546: \t\t\tCode: int(resp.ExitCode),\n547: \t\t}\n548: \t}\n549: \n550: \treturn resp.Stdout, resp.Stderr, err\n551: }\n552: \n553: // Exec prepares a streaming endpoint grpc.NewClient() accepts the same DialOption types\n   - Existing dial options continue to work without modification\n\n2. **Context Management**:\n   - Context timeout should be managed via dial options (grpc.WithConnectParams)\n   - Cancellation context is not applicable to grpc.NewClient\n\n3. **Code Organization**:\n   - Non-vendor files only (as per requirements)\n   - Proto definitions and generated code excluded\n   - Test files included where deprecated calls are used\n\n### File Locations\n\n#### Source Code Repositories\n- **Base Path**: `/workspace/ccb_crossrepo/src/`\n- **Subdirectories**:\n  -
